    dependency: dependent
  - name: P_E
    type: rational
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  - name: m_n
    type: tuple
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: out
    type: string
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - n = 7.
  - S is chosen uniformly among 3-subsets of {1,2,3,4,5,6,7}, written S={s1<s2<s3}.
  - A_S is the 3x3 real matrix with top two rows [[0,1,0],[0,0,1]] and bottom row [(-1)^{s1}, s2-4, (-1)^{s3}].
  - v is chosen uniformly from {0,1}^3 \ {0}.
  - X(t) solves X'(t)=A_S X(t), X(0)=v.
  - W(t)=det[X(t), X'(t), X''(t)].
  - T is chosen uniformly among 3-subsets of {0,1,2,3,4,5,6}.
  - q_T(z)=\prod_{t in T}(z-t) in Z[z].
  - Theta_T=\sum_{k=0}^4 q_T(k).
  - 'Event E: (i) W(t)\neq0 for all t\geq0, and (ii) 6 | Theta_T.'
  ask:
  - Compute P(E)=m/n in lowest terms and output the zero-padded three-digit string for ((m+2n) mod 1000).
answer_spec:
  type: string
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: universe_size
    type: int
    domain: integer >= 3
    purpose: size of the base set for S and T (here 7)
  - name: subset_size_S
    type: int
    domain: integer >= 1
    purpose: size of S defining A_S (here 3)
  - name: matrix_template
    type: symbolic_recipe
    domain: companion_like_with_discrete_parameters
    purpose: maps S to A_S via parity signs and a centered shift beta=s2-4
  - name: initial_vector_distribution
    type: discrete_distribution
    domain: nonzero_binary_vectors_dimension_3
    purpose: chooses v uniformly from {0,1}^3\{0}
  - name: subset_size_T
    type: int
    domain: integer >= 1
    purpose: size of T defining q_T (here 3)
  - name: evaluation_points
    type: list_int
    domain: fixed_small_integers
    purpose: k=0..4 used in Theta_T
  - name: divisibility_modulus
    type: int
    domain: positive_integer
    purpose: modulus 6 for Theta_T condition
  - name: output_modulus
    type: int
    domain: positive_integer
    purpose: 1000 for final reduction
  - name: output_linear_form
    type: other
    domain: (m+2n)
    purpose: postprocessing uses the linear form m+2n before reducing mod 1000
  invariant(s):
  - name: wronskian_reduction_to_static_determinant
    depends_on:
    - A_S
    - v
    statement: For X'=A_S X with X(t)=e^{tA_S}v, we have X^{(k)}(t)=A_S^k X(t), hence W(t)=det[X,AX,A^2X]=det(e^{tA_S}) \cdot det[v,Av,A^2v]=e^{t \cdot tr(A_S)} \cdot Delta_S(v). Therefore W(t)\neq0 for all t\geq0 iff Delta_S(v)\neq0.
  - name: event_independence_factorization
    depends_on:
    - S
    - v
    - T
    statement: (S,v) are independent of T, and the W-condition depends only on (S,v) while the Theta-condition depends only on T, so P(E)=P(Delta_S(v)\neq0) \cdot P(6|Theta_T).
  - name: theta_sum_closed_form
    depends_on:
    - T
    - evaluation_points
    statement: Writing q_T(z)=z^3-s1 z^2+s2 z-s3, we have Theta_T=\sum_{k=0}^4 q_T(k)=100-30s1+10s2-5s3.
  - name: theta_mod6_structure
    depends_on:
    - T
    statement: From Theta_T=100-30s1+10s2-5s3, the mod-2 condition reduces to s3 even (i.e., T contains an even element). Mod 3, Theta_T \equiv 1+s2+s3, which depends only on the residues of elements of T modulo 3.
  operator(s):
  - build_matrix_from_subset
  - solve_linear_system_via_matrix_exponential
  - relate_higher_derivatives_to_powers_of_A
  - determinant_multiplicativity
  - trace_exponential_identity
  - finite_enumeration_over_subsets
  - polynomial_from_roots
  - symmetric_sum_expansion
  - modular_reduction_mod_2_and_mod_3
  - gcd_fraction_reduction
  - residue_and_zero_padding
  essential_assumption(s):
  - The ODE solution exists uniquely for all t\geq0 (true for constant-coefficient linear systems).
  - 'Determinant and trace identities over reals apply: det(e^{tA})=e^{t tr(A)}.'
  - Uniform distributions on S, v, and T are independent.
  - Theta_T is interpreted as an integer; divisibility is in Z.
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - 'The finite sample spaces have fixed sizes: |C(7,3)|=35 for S and T, and 7 for v. The W-condition reduces deterministically to Delta_S(v)\neq0, so counting good pairs (S,v) gives a unique rational. Separately, counting T with 6|Theta_T gives a unique rational. Independence fixes P(E) uniquely; lowest-terms reduction fixes (m,n) uniquely and thus the three-digit residue of (m+2n) mod 1000.'
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: wronskian_to_initial_data_determinant
    inputs:
    - A_S
    - v
    purpose: replace the infinite-time condition W(t)\neq0 for all t\geq0 by the finite check Delta_S(v)\neq0
    outputs:
    - Delta_S(v)
  - name: discrete_parameterization_of_A_S
    inputs:
    - S
    purpose: encode A_S by (alpha,beta,gamma) with alpha=(-1)^{s1}, beta=s2-4, gamma=(-1)^{s3}
    outputs:
    - alpha
    - beta
    - gamma
  - name: enumerate_zero_locus_over_binary_vectors
    inputs:
    - alpha
    - beta
    - gamma
    purpose: evaluate Delta_S(v) on the 7 nonzero binary vectors and count vanishing cases
    outputs:
    - count_good_pairs_Sv
  - name: expand_theta_via_symmetric_sums
    inputs:
    - T
    purpose: express Theta_T via symmetric sums and precomputed power sums over k=0..4, then reduce mod 6
    outputs:
    - Theta_T_mod6
  - name: classify_T_by_residues_mod3
    inputs:
    - T
    purpose: count T satisfying the mod-3 component by residue-class patterns, then combine with the parity constraint
    outputs:
    - count_good_T
  - name: multiply_probabilities_and_postprocess
    inputs:
    - P_Delta
    - P_Theta
    purpose: compute P(E), reduce to m/n, then output zero-padded ((m+2n) mod 1000)
    outputs:
    - out
  theorem(s):
  - name: matrix_exponential_solution
    role: X(t)=e^{tA}v for constant A
  - name: derivative_power_relation
    role: X^{(k)}=A^k X for X'=AX
  - name: determinant_multiplicativity_and_trace_exponential
    role: det(e^{tA})=e^{t tr(A)} and det(e^{tA}B)=det(e^{tA})det(B)
  - name: polynomial_from_roots_expansion
    role: q_T(z)=\prod(z-t)=z^3-s1 z^2+s2 z-s3
  - name: CRT_style_mod6_check
    role: 6|Theta ⇔ (2|Theta and 3|Theta)
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - reduce an analytic condition to a finite algebraic determinant, then do exact finite counting
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - Use W(t)=e^{t tr(A_S)}\Delta_S(v) to turn "for all t" into an algebraic determinant condition at t=0.
  - Count (S,v) by classifying A_S via discrete parameters (α,β,γ) and checking \Delta_S(v) on the 7 binary vectors.
  - Compute Θ_T mod 6 via symmetric-sum expansion and residue-pattern counting modulo 3, with a simple parity exclusion.
  - Multiply the independent probabilities, reduce to lowest terms, then compute the required three-digit residue of (m+2n) mod 1000.
  finish_type: exact_probability_by_enumeration
computational_context:
  preferred_language: python
  library(s):
  - name: itertools
    reason: enumerate subsets S,T and vectors v
  - name: fractions
    reason: exact rational arithmetic and reduction
  - name: math
    reason: gcd for normalization and modular postprocessing
  import_hint(s):
  - from itertools import combinations, product
  - from fractions import Fraction
  - from math import gcd
  task(s):
  - name: brute_force_count_Delta_nonzero
    reason: enumerate all 35 subsets S and 7 vectors v, compute Delta_S(v)=det[v,Av,A^2v], count nonzero
  - name: brute_force_count_Theta_divisible_by_6
    reason: enumerate all 35 subsets T, compute Theta_T=\sum_{k=0}^4 \prod_{t\inT}(k-t), count those divisible by 6
  - name: verify_independence_product
    reason: confirm P(E) equals the product of the two independent proportions
  - name: format_three_digit_output
    reason: compute (m+2n)%1000 and zero-pad to length 3
well_posed: true
instance_snapshot:
  parameter_value(s):
    n: 7
    subset_size_S: 3
    subset_size_T: 3
    evaluation_points:
    - 0
    - 1
    - 2
    - 3
    - 4
    divisibility_modulus: 6
    output_modulus: 1000
  final_answer:
    value: '647'
    normalized: '647'

source_short_name: $p$-adic valuation algebra (sum/product rules, compare $v_p$) - Buffed Seed
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: p_adic_valuation__basic_rules__vp_of_sum_product__buffed
  instance_id: imocompendium:Def_exact_power_of_p_Thm2.126
  short_name: $p$-adic valuation algebra (sum/product rules, compare $v_p$) - Buffed Seed
  generation: 2
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - p-adic valuations
  - divisibility
  topic_path:
  - p_adic_valuations
  - valuation_rules
  - vp_sum_min_rule
  tags:
  - compare_vp_on_both_sides
  - exact_power_definition
  - valuation_comparisons
  - valuation_invariant
  - valuation_vp_definition
  - vp_product
  - vp_quotient
  - vp_sum_exact_when_unequal
  - vp_sum_lower_bound
  - sterilized
  - buffed
  - gymnasium_ready
  - two_regime_interaction
  - order_lifting
  - prime_power_unit_group
problem_spec:
  summary: Let $p$ and $q$ be distinct odd primes and let $x,y$ be nonzero integers with $v_p(x)=v_p(y)$ and $v_q(x)\ne v_q(y)$. Write $a:=v_p(x)=v_p(y)$, set $x=p^a u$ and $y=p^a v$ with $p\nmid uv$, and fix an integer $k\ge 2$; consider the unit $r\equiv u v^{-1}\pmod{p^{k+1}}$. Assume that $r$ has multiplicative order $2$ modulo $p^k$ but does not have order $2$ modulo $p^{k+1}$.
  object(s):
  - name: p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: k
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: x
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: y
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: a
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: u
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: v
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: r
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: t
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: N
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes.
  - $k\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $k\ge 2$.
  - $x,y\in\mathbb{Z}\setminus\{0\}$ and $v_p(x)=v_p(y)=:a\ge 1$.
  - Write $x=p^a u$ and $y=p^a v$ with $p\nmid uv$, and set $r\equiv u\,v^{-1}\pmod{p^{k+1}}$ (so $r\in(\mathbb{Z}/p^{k+1}\mathbb{Z})^\times$).
  - The class $r\pmod{p^k}$ has multiplicative order $2$, but $r\pmod{p^{k+1}}$ does not have order $2$ (equivalently, $r\equiv -1\pmod{p^k}$ and $r\not\equiv -1\pmod{p^{k+1}}$).
  - 'At the second prime, the valuations are distinct and nontrivial: $v_q(x)$ and $v_q(y)$ are positive integers with $v_q(x)\ne v_q(y)$.'
  ask:
  - Stage 1 (order). Let $t:=\operatorname{ord}_{p^{k+1}}(r)$. Prove that $t$ is uniquely determined by the givens and compute $t$ explicitly.
  - Stage 2 (valuation). Using the value of $t$, compute $$N := v_p\bigl(x^{t/2}+y^{t/2}\bigr) + v_q(x+y)$$ and report the final answer as the unique integer in $\{0,1,\dots,999\}$ congruent to $N\pmod{1000}$.
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p\ge 3$
    purpose: prime governing the $p$-adic valuation and the unit group $(\mathbb{Z}/p^{k+1}\mathbb{Z})^\times$
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $q\ge 3$ with $q\ne p$
    purpose: second prime used to force an independent unequal-valuation constraint at $q$
  - name: k
    type: int
    domain: integer $k\ge 2$
    purpose: 'structural depth parameter: forces a nontrivial order-lifting step from $p^k$ to $p^{k+1}$'
  - name: x
    type: int
    domain: nonzero integer $x$ with $v_p(x)\ge 1$ and $v_q(x)\ge 1$
    purpose: first integer (carries both $p$- and $q$-adic divisibility data)
  - name: y
    type: int
    domain: nonzero integer $y$ with $v_p(y)=v_p(x)$ and $v_q(y)\ge 1$ but $v_q(y)\ne v_q(x)$
    purpose: second integer (shares the $p$-adic valuation with $x$ but differs at $q$)
  invariant(s):
  - name: anti_triviality_distinct_odd_primes
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    statement: $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes (in particular $p\ne 2$), avoiding $2$-adic exceptional behavior and ensuring a unique element of order $2$ in $(\mathbb{Z}/p^n\mathbb{Z})^\times$ for all $n\ge 1$.
  - name: anti_triviality_depth_and_equal_p_valuation
    depends_on:
    - p
    - k
    - x
    - y
    statement: The instance enforces $k\ge 2$ and $v_p(x)=v_p(y)=a\ge 1$, so the one-step unequal-valuation minimum rule at $p$ does not apply and a genuine lifting argument at a prime power modulus is required.
  - name: valuation_additivity
    depends_on:
    - x
    - y
    - p
    statement: For nonzero integers, $v_p(xy)=v_p(x)+v_p(y)$ and $v_p(x/y)=v_p(x)-v_p(y)$ when $y\mid x$ in $\mathbb{Z}_{(p)}$.
  - name: min_rule_for_sum_when_unequal
    depends_on:
    - x
    - y
    - q
    statement: If $v_q(x)\ne v_q(y)$ then $v_q(x+y)=\min\{v_q(x),v_q(y)\}$.
  - name: order_2_characterizes_minus_one_mod_p_power
    depends_on:
    - p
    - k
    statement: For odd prime $p$, the unique element of order $2$ in $(\mathbb{Z}/p^k\mathbb{Z})^\times$ is $-1\bmod p^k$. Thus $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(r)=2$ forces $r\equiv -1\pmod{p^k}$.
  - name: order_lift_from_pk_to_pk1_in_minus_one_coset
    depends_on:
    - p
    - k
    statement: If $r\equiv -1\pmod{p^k}$ but $r\not\equiv -1\pmod{p^{k+1}}$ with odd prime $p$, then $\operatorname{ord}_{p^{k+1}}(r)=2p$ (the order multiplies by $p$ in this lift).
  - name: vp_of_power_sum_from_order
    depends_on:
    - p
    - k
    - x
    - y
    statement: With $a=v_p(x)=v_p(y)$, $x=p^a u$, $y=p^a v$ and $r\equiv u v^{-1}\pmod{p^{k+1}}$, if $t=\operatorname{ord}_{p^{k+1}}(r)=2p$ then $r^{t/2}=r^{p}\equiv -1\pmod{p^{k+1}}$ and $v_p(u+v)=k$, hence (by LTE) $v_p(u^{t/2}+v^{t/2})=k+1$; therefore $v_p(x^{t/2}+y^{t/2})=a\cdot \frac t2 + (k+1)$.
  operator(s):
  - valuation_vp_definition
  - vp_product
  - vp_quotient
  - vp_sum_lower_bound
  - vp_sum_exact_when_unequal
  - compare_vp_on_both_sides
  - valuation_invariant
  - multiplicative_order_mod_prime_power
  - unit_factorization_x_equals_p_power_times_unit
  - order_lifting_prime_power
  - vp_of_power_sum_via_factorization
  essential_assumption(s):
  - Instances are forced into the equal-$p$-valuation regime $v_p(x)=v_p(y)$ (with $k\ge 2$), so the standard one-step template $v_p(x+y)=\min\{v_p(x),v_p(y)\}$ cannot be used at $p$.
  - 'The order constraint on the unit ratio $r\equiv (x/p^{v_p(x)})(y/p^{v_p(y)})^{-1}\pmod{p^{k+1}}$ is essential: it pins down the exact lift $t=\operatorname{ord}_{p^{k+1}}(r)$ and therefore forces $v_p(x^{t/2}+y^{t/2})$; without it, the $p$-adic valuation of the power-sum can jump and the output would not be well-defined from the stated data.'
  - 'Normalization is part of the task contract: the computed integer is reported modulo $1000$ to obtain a unique output in $\{0,1,\dots,999\}$.'
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - The condition $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(r)=2$ forces $r\equiv -1\pmod{p^k}$, while the explicit exclusion of order $2$ modulo $p^{k+1}$ forces the unique lift $\operatorname{ord}_{p^{k+1}}(r)=2p$, so Stage 1 has a single outcome.
  - Given $t$, the value $v_p(x^{t/2}+y^{t/2})$ is forced by factoring out $p^{a\cdot t/2}$ and using the induced congruence $r^{t/2}\equiv -1\pmod{p^{k+1}}$ together with LTE to obtain an exact exponent; independently, $v_q(x+y)$ is forced by the unequal-valuation minimum rule at $q$.
  - Therefore $N$ is uniquely determined as an integer, and the normalization rule $N\mapsto N\bmod 1000$ yields a unique final answer in $\{0,1,\dots,999\}$.
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: valuation_vp_definition
    inputs:
    - integer $n\ne 0$ and prime $p$
    purpose: encode divisibility by prime powers as an exponent and enable conversion between congruences/divisibility and valuation inequalities
    outputs:
    - the integer $v_p(n)$ such that $p^{v_p(n)}\mid n$ but $p^{v_p(n)+1}\nmid n$
  - name: vp_product
    inputs:
    - $x,y$
    purpose: linearize multiplicative divisibility constraints after factoring common prime powers
    outputs:
    - $v_p(xy)=v_p(x)+v_p(y)$
  - name: vp_sum_exact_when_unequal_at_q
    inputs:
    - $x,y$ with $v_q(x)\ne v_q(y)$
    purpose: compute $v_q(x+y)$ exactly via the unequal-valuation minimum rule (secondary prime valuation constraint)
    outputs:
    - $v_q(x+y)=\min\{v_q(x),v_q(y)\}$
  - name: order_in_unit_group_mod_prime_power
    inputs:
    - odd prime $p$, level $n\ge 1$, and a unit class $r\in (\mathbb{Z}/p^n\mathbb{Z})^\times$
    purpose: use multiplicative order (ORDER regime) to translate order information into rigid congruences such as $r\equiv -1\pmod{p^n}$ when the order is $2$
    outputs:
    - rigid congruence information about $r$ forced by its order modulo $p^n$
  - name: order_lifting_from_pk_to_pk1
    inputs:
    - unit $r\equiv -1\pmod{p^k}$ with $r\not\equiv -1\pmod{p^{k+1}}$
    purpose: determine the lifted multiplicative order $\operatorname{ord}_{p^{k+1}}(r)$; this ORDER step is what makes the valuation computation well-posed
    outputs:
    - $\operatorname{ord}_{p^{k+1}}(r)=2p$
  - name: order_to_vp_power_sum_interaction
    inputs:
    - the computed order $t=\operatorname{ord}_{p^{k+1}}(r)$ and the factorization $x=p^a u,\ y=p^a v$
    purpose: 'interaction kernel (VP + ORDER): use $r^{t/2}\equiv -1\pmod{p^{k+1}}$ to force an exact $p$-adic valuation of the power-sum $u^{t/2}+v^{t/2}$ (via LTE), then lift it back to $v_p(x^{t/2}+y^{t/2})$ by valuation additivity'
    outputs:
    - a closed-form expression for $v_p(x^{t/2}+y^{t/2})$ in terms of $a,k,t$
  - name: compare_vp_on_both_sides
    inputs:
    - an identity or divisibility relation
    purpose: compare valuations to isolate unknown exponents (e.g., showing an order cannot be $2$ by comparing $v_p(r^2-1)$ at successive levels)
    outputs:
    - inequalities/equalities of integer exponents coming from valuations
  theorem(s):
  - name: exact_division_arguments
    role: translate "$p^t\mid n$" into "$v_p(n)\ge t$" and vice versa; used to pass between congruences and valuations.
  - name: cyclic_unit_group_mod_odd_prime_power
    role: for odd prime $p$, the group $(\mathbb{Z}/p^n\mathbb{Z})^\times$ is cyclic; in particular the element of order $2$ is uniquely $-1$.
  - name: binomial_lift_of_order
    role: if $r\equiv -1\pmod{p^k}$ but not modulo $p^{k+1}$, then $r^2\equiv 1+cp^k\pmod{p^{k+1}}$ with $p\nmid c$, so $(r^2)^p\equiv 1\pmod{p^{k+1}}$ while $r^2\not\equiv 1\pmod{p^{k+1}}$, forcing order $2p$.
  - name: LTE_for_u^p_plus_v^p
    role: for odd prime $p$ with $p\mid(u+v)$ and $p\nmid uv$, one has $v_p(u^p+v^p)=v_p(u+v)+1$; used to get the exact exponent $k+1$ in the power-sum valuation.
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - Divisibility by prime powers is controlled by a single integer exponent; multiplicative order constraints in unit groups modulo prime powers rigidify congruences, which then feed into valuation bookkeeping on power-sums.
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - Factor $x=p^a u$ and $y=p^a v$ to separate the common $p$-power from $p$-units, and encode the unit ratio $r\equiv u v^{-1}\pmod{p^{k+1}}$.
  - Use that the unique element of order $2$ in $(\mathbb{Z}/p^k\mathbb{Z})^\times$ is $-1$ and lift the order from $p^k$ to $p^{k+1}$ to force $t=2p$; this ORDER step is what makes the $p$-adic valuation computation rigid.
  - Translate the congruence $r^{t/2}\equiv -1\pmod{p^{k+1}}$ into an exact valuation for $u^{t/2}+v^{t/2}$ via LTE, then use valuation additivity to obtain $v_p(x^{t/2}+y^{t/2})$; compute $v_q(x+y)$ separately by the unequal-valuation minimum rule and finally reduce modulo $1000$.
  finish_type: closed_form
computational_context:
  preferred_language: python
  library(s):
  - name: math
    reason: 'Core integer arithmetic: gcd, lcm, modular exponentiation scaffolding.'
  - name: sympy
    reason: 'Symbolic/number theory utilities: factorint, primerange, mod_inverse, crt; useful for verification tasks.'
  import_hint(s):
  - import math
  - from sympy import factorint, crt, mod_inverse
  task(s):
  - name: primary_structural_check
    reason: Validate that the produced object(s) satisfy all defining congruences/divisibility constraints for a deterministic sweep of small admissible parameter tuples (e.g., the first few odd primes and bounded |x|,|y| satisfying the invariants).
  - name: secondary_independent_check
    reason: Cross-check the main result via an alternative computation (e.g., brute force for small bounds, or an independent formula such as order computation vs. valuation computation).
  - name: vp_rule_enumerative_check
    reason: Enumerate small primes and integers in a fixed bounded range, compute $v_p$ by repeated division, and verify the stated product/sum rules.
  - name: order_lift_and_power_sum_check
    reason: For a deterministic collection of admissible instances, compute $r=(x/p^a)(y/p^a)^{-1}\bmod p^{k+1}$, verify $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(r)=2$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{p^{k+1}}(r)=2p$, then numerically check the predicted valuations $v_p(x^{t/2}+y^{t/2})$ and $v_q(x+y)$.
well_posed: 'yes'
instance_snapshot:
  final_answer:
    value: 744
    normalized: 744

source_short_name: Binomial coefficients mod $p$ and $v_p\binom{n}{k}$ (Lucas/Legendre/Kummer) - Buffed Seed
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: binomial_prime_valuation__Lucas_Kummer__factorial_divisibility__buffed
  instance_id: imocompendium:3.23.2_Longlisted_AUS1_factorial_binomial_integrality
  short_name: Binomial coefficients mod $p$ and $v_p\binom{n}{k}$ (Lucas/Legendre/Kummer) - Buffed Seed
  generation: 2
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - factorials & binomials
  - p-adic valuation
  - combinatorial number theory
  topic_path:
  - factorials_binomials
  - binomial_mod_p
  - Lucas_and_Kummer
  tags:
  - Kummer_theorem_optional
  - Legendre_formula
  - Lucas_theorem
  - base_p_digit_decomposition
  - binomial_mod_p_patterns
  - carry_count_interpretation
  - central_binomial_properties
  - combinatorial_count_as_integer
  - combinatorial_invariants_for_divisibility
  - counting_paths_as_integer_optional
  - divisibility_in_sums_of_binomials
  - factorial_congruences_optional
  - gcd_binomial_coefficients
  - vp_binomial
  - vp_factorial
  - vp_multinomial_optional
  - vp_n_factorial
  - sterilized
  - buffed
  - gymnasium_ready
  - two_regime_interaction
problem_spec:
  summary: Let $p$ be an odd prime and let $n$ be a positive integer whose base-$p$ expansion ends with a block of $t\ge1$ digits equal to $p-1$, followed by a digit $a\in\{0,1,\dots,p-2\}$, followed by a block of $s\ge1$ zeros, and then a nonzero digit $b\in\{1,2,\dots,p-1\}$. Consider the sequence $A_k=\binom{n}{k}$ for $0\le k\le n$. Define $k^*$ to be the smallest index $1\le k\le n-1$ for which $p\mid A_k$, and put $e=v_p(A_{k^*})$.
  object(s):
  - name: p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: t
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: s
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: a
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: b
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: u
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: n
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: A_k
    type: sequence
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k^*
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: e
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: N
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - $p\ge5$ is an odd prime
  - $t\ge1$ and $s\ge1$ are integers
  - $a\in\{0,1,\dots,p-2\}$ and $b\in\{1,2,\dots,p-1\}$
  - $u\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge0}$
  - Let $n=u\,p^{t+s+2}+b\,p^{t+s+1}+a\,p^{t}+(p^{t}-1)$ (equivalently, the base-$p$ digits of $n$ are $(p-1,\dots,p-1,a,\underbrace{0,\dots,0}_{s\text{ zeros}},b,\text{digits of }u)$ from least to most significant).
  - 'For $0\le k\le n$, define $A_k=\binom{n}{k}$. Let $k^*=\min\{1\le k\le n-1: p\mid A_k\}$ and $e=v_p(A_{k^*})$.'
  ask:
  - (i) Prove that $k^*$ exists and is uniquely determined by the above digit pattern of $n$, and show that in fact $k^*=(a+1)p^{t}$.
  - (ii) Compute $N\equiv k^*+p^{e}\pmod{1000}$, and report the unique integer $N\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$.
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p\ge5$
    purpose: base for Lucas/Kummer; determines the digit system and the $p$-adic valuation
  - name: t
    type: int
    domain: integer $t\ge1$
    purpose: length of the trailing $(p-1)$-digit block in the base-$p$ expansion of $n$; forces the location of the first Lucas-vanishing digit
  - name: s
    type: int
    domain: integer $s\ge1$
    purpose: length of the zero block after digit $a$; forces a nontrivial carry chain of length $s+1$ in Kummer carry counting
  - name: a
    type: int
    domain: integer $0\le a\le p-2$
    purpose: the first base-$p$ digit of $n$ (from the least significant side) that is not equal to $p-1$
  - name: b
    type: int
    domain: integer $1\le b\le p-1$
    purpose: a nonzero base-$p$ digit above the zero block; ensures $k^*<n$ and terminates the carry chain
  - name: u
    type: int
    domain: integer $u\ge0$
    purpose: higher base-$p$ digits of $n$ (above the controlled suffix); allows structural variation without changing the suffix interface
  invariant(s):
  - name: structured_base_p_suffix
    depends_on:
    - p
    - t
    - s
    - a
    - b
    - u
    statement: The integer $n=u\,p^{t+s+2}+b\,p^{t+s+1}+a\,p^{t}+(p^{t}-1)$ has base-$p$ suffix $(\underbrace{p-1,\dots,p-1}_{t\text{ digits}},a,\underbrace{0,\dots,0}_{s\text{ zeros}},b)$ (least to most significant within the suffix), with $a\le p-2$ and $b\ge1$.
  - name: anti_triviality_guards_1
    depends_on:
    - p
    - t
    - s
    statement: 'Non-degeneracy: $p\ge5$, $t\ge1$, and $s\ge1$, so the row index $n\ge p^{t+s+1}$ and the carry count in the valuation step is at least $2$.'
  - name: anti_triviality_guards_2
    depends_on:
    - a
    - b
    statement: 'Non-degeneracy: $a\in\{0,1,\dots,p-2\}$ (so the first base-$p$ digit of $n$ that is not $p-1$ occurs at position $t$) and $b\in\{1,2,\dots,p-1\}$ (so $k^*<n$ and the carry chain terminates).'
  - name: Lucas_nonvanishing_criterion
    depends_on:
    - p
    - n
    - k
    statement: For $0\le k\le n$, Lucas' theorem implies $\binom{n}{k}\not\equiv 0\pmod p$ iff every base-$p$ digit of $k$ is at most the corresponding digit of $n$.
  - name: Kummer_carry_valuation
    depends_on:
    - p
    - n
    - k
    statement: 'Kummer''s carry interpretation: for $0\le k\le n$, $v_p\!\left(\binom{n}{k}\right)$ equals the number of carries when adding $k$ and $n-k$ in base $p$.'
  - name: forced_carry_chain_for_kstar
    depends_on:
    - p
    - t
    - s
    - a
    - b
    statement: For $k^*=(a+1)p^{t}$, subtracting $k^*$ from $n$ triggers a borrow at digit-position $t$ that propagates through exactly the $s$ consecutive zero digits above it and stops at the digit $b\ge1$; hence the base-$p$ addition $k^*+(n-k^*)$ produces exactly $s+1$ carries.
  operator(s):
  - vp_n_factorial
  - vp_factorial
  - Legendre_formula
  - vp_binomial
  - vp_multinomial_optional
  - Lucas_theorem
  - binomial_mod_p_patterns
  - Kummer_theorem_optional
  - central_binomial_properties
  - gcd_binomial_coefficients
  - divisibility_in_sums_of_binomials
  - combinatorial_count_as_integer
  - combinatorial_invariants_for_divisibility
  - counting_paths_as_integer_optional
  - factorial_congruences_optional
  - carry_propagation_in_base_p
  - minimal_index_operator
  - mod_1000_reduction
  essential_assumption(s):
  - Digits are taken in base $p$; the instance must keep $p$ prime to use Lucas/Kummer in their standard forms.
  - The index $k^*$ is defined implicitly (as a minimality object); solving an instance requires first using Lucas' digitwise nonvanishing criterion to determine $k^*$, and only then using a $p$-adic valuation method (Kummer carries / Legendre) to determine $e$-a direct one-shot evaluation of a fixed $\binom{n}{k}$ is intentionally blocked by the size/structure guards.
  - The reported output is the canonical representative of an integer modulo $1000$ in $\{0,1,\dots,999\}$; intermediate quantities may be larger but must be reduced deterministically at the end.
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - Lucas' theorem makes the predicate $p\mid\binom{n}{k}$ equivalent to a base-$p$ digit inequality, so the least such $k^*$ is forced by the controlled suffix of $n$ (hence existence and uniqueness of $k^*$ under the guards).
  - Kummer's carry theorem then forces $e=v_p\!\left(\binom{n}{k^*}\right)$ as an exact carry count determined by the same digit pattern, so $N\equiv k^*+p^e\pmod{1000}$ is uniquely determined.
  - 'Normalization: reduce modulo $1000$ and select the canonical representative in $\{0,1,\dots,999\}$.'
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: Lucas_theorem
    inputs:
    - base-$p$ digits of $n$ and a variable index $k$
    purpose: Characterize when $\binom{n}{k}\equiv0\pmod p$ and locate the first index $k^*$ where this vanishing occurs under the structured digit suffix (BINOM regime).
    outputs:
    - closed-form identification of the minimal $k^*$ from digit constraints
  - name: v_p_via_Kummer_carries
    inputs:
    - base-$p$ subtraction/addition for $k^*$ and $n-k^*$
    purpose: Compute $e=v_p\!\left(\binom{n}{k^*}\right)$ by counting carries; the length of the forced borrow/carry chain is read off from the same digit block that defined $k^*$ (VP regime).
    outputs:
    - exact value of $e$ as a carry count (here, forced to equal $s+1$)
  - name: Lucas_Kummer_interface_step
    inputs:
    - the structured base-$p$ suffix of $n$
    purpose: 'Interaction kernel: use Lucas to pin down $k^*$ via the first digit where $k$ must exceed the corresponding digit of $n$, then use Kummer carry counting on that specific $k^*$ to obtain $e$ for the modulus-$1000$ computation.'
    outputs:
    - two-phase pipeline $(n\text{ digits})\Rightarrow k^*\Rightarrow e$ that determines $N$
  - name: mod_1000_reduction
    inputs:
    - integers $k^*$, $p$, and $e$
    purpose: Compute $k^*+p^e$ modulo $1000$ using deterministic modular arithmetic.
    outputs:
    - the normalized answer $N\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$
  theorem(s):
  - name: combinatorial_count_as_integer
    role: justify integrality of expressions by counting interpretations (often used to prove divisibility)
  - name: Lucas_theorem
    role: digitwise evaluation of binomial coefficients modulo a prime
  - name: Kummer_theorem
    role: interprets $v_p\binom{n}{k}$ as the number of carries in base $p$
  - name: Legendre_formula
    role: alternative floor-sum computation of $v_p(n!)$ for cross-checking the valuation
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - Base-$p$ expansions simultaneously control (i) vanishing/nonvanishing of $\binom{n}{k}\bmod p$ (Lucas) and (ii) accumulation of $p$-powers in factorial ratios via carries (Kummer), enabling a coupled minimality+valuation determination.
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - 'Use the shared interface "base-$p$ digits of $n$ and $k$": Lucas gives the mod $p$ residue and Kummer/Legendre gives the $p$-adic valuation.'
  - For sum-of-binomial divisibility variants, interpret the sum as counting objects modulo $p$ or track valuation term-by-term.
  - 'Exploit the engineered base-$p$ suffix of $n$: Lucas forces the first vanishing coefficient to occur at $k^*=(a+1)p^{t}$, and the block of $s$ zeros forces exactly $s+1$ carries in Kummer''s theorem, giving $e=s+1$.'
  - After determining $(k^*,e)$, compute $k^*+p^e$ and normalize by reduction modulo $1000$ into $\{0,1,\dots,999\}$.
  finish_type: existence_uniqueness
computational_context:
  preferred_language: python
  library(s):
  - name: math
    reason: 'Core integer arithmetic: gcd, lcm, modular exponentiation scaffolding.'
  - name: sympy
    reason: 'Optional symbolic/number theory utilities: factorint, primerange, mod_inverse, crt; useful for verification tasks.'
  import_hint(s):
  - import math
  - from sympy import factorint, crt, mod_inverse
  task(s):
  - name: digit_suffix_sanity_check
    reason: Verify that sampled parameters produce an $n$ whose base-$p$ suffix matches the required pattern and that $1\le k^*\le n-1$.
  - name: lucas_minimality_check
    reason: Verify (via digitwise inequality, not brute force) that $\binom{n}{k}\not\equiv0\pmod p$ for all $0<k<k^*$ and that $\binom{n}{k^*}\equiv0\pmod p$.
  - name: carry_count_vs_legendre_crosscheck
    reason: Independently compute $v_p\binom{n}{k^*}$ via (i) carry counting and (ii) Legendre's floor-sum formula for factorial valuations to validate the VP step.
well_posed: 'yes'
instance_snapshot:
  final_answer:
    value: 957
    normalized: 957

source_short_name: CRT exponent targets with 2-adic and Hensel-lifted quartic residue coupling
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: CRT__2power_times_prime_power_times_prime_square_times_prime__hensel_quartic_target__four_exponent_constraints
  instance_id: nt_02_gen4_instance_2026-02-25__nonce_a7c2_nt_02_4
  short_name: CRT exponent targets with 2-adic and Hensel-lifted quartic residue coupling
  generation: 4
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - congruences
  - CRT
  - multiplicative_order
  - primitive_roots
  - prime_powers
  - prime_squares
  - p-adic_valuations
  - 2-adic_units
  - LTE
  - Hensel_lifting
  - quadratic_residues
  - linear_congruences
  - modular_exponentiation
  topic_path:
  - modular_arithmetic
  - Chinese_remainder
  - exponent_congruences
  - hensel_lifting_and_unit_orders
  tags:
  - CRT_synthesis
  - LTE_order_odd_prime_power
  - LTE_order_two_power
  - hensel_lift_square_root_minus_one
  - order_four_classification
  - gcd_coupling_constraints
  - staged_congruence_solving
  - normalize_mod_999
problem_spec:
  summary: Construct a unit A by CRT modulo 2^m p^k q^2 ell from residues A \equiv u (mod 2^m), A \equiv 1+p (mod p^k), A \equiv g (mod q^2), A \equiv h (mod ell). Define a 2-adic target D \equiv u^t (mod 2^m), a Hensel-lifted quartic target B (the unique lift to q^2 of a chosen square root of -1 mod q), and an auxiliary prime target C \equiv h^{(ell-1)/2} (mod ell). Translate A^n congruences into a coupled exponent system with non-coprime moduli, solve by gcd reduction / sequential CRT, then normalize n0 modulo 999.
  object(s):
  - name: m
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: k
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: ell
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: u
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: g
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: h
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: t
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: x0
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: D
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: B
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: C
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: A
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: n0
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  - name: N
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - $m \ge 3$.
  - $p,q,\ell$ are pairwise distinct odd primes, $k \ge 3$, and $q \equiv 1 \pmod 4$.
  - $u$ is odd with $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(u)=2^{m-2}$.
  - $g$ is a primitive root modulo $q^2$, hence $\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(g)=q(q-1)$.
  - $h$ is a primitive root modulo $\ell$, hence $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell}(h)=\ell-1$.
  - $t$ is an integer with $0 \le t < 2^{m-2}$.
  - $x_0$ is an integer with $x_0^2 \equiv -1 \pmod q$ and $q \nmid x_0$.
  - $D$ is the least nonnegative integer with $D \equiv u^{t} \pmod{2^m}$.
  - $B$ is the least nonnegative integer with $B \equiv x_0 \pmod q$ and $B^2 \equiv -1 \pmod{q^2}$.
  - $C$ is the least nonnegative integer with $C \equiv h^{(\ell-1)/2} \pmod{\ell}$.
  - $A$ is the least nonnegative integer with $A \equiv u \pmod{2^m}$, $A \equiv 1+p \pmod{p^k}$, $A \equiv g \pmod{q^2}$, and $A \equiv h \pmod{\ell}$.
  ask:
  - Determine the unique integer $N$ with $0 \le N < 999$ and $N \equiv n_0 \pmod{999}$, where $n_0$ is the least positive $n$ such that $A^n \equiv D \pmod{2^m}$, $A^n \equiv 1 \pmod{p^k}$, $A^n \equiv B \pmod{q^2}$, and $A^n \equiv C \pmod{\ell}$.
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 999
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: m
    type: int
    domain: integer $m \ge 3$
    purpose: supplies a 2-adic modulus $2^m$ whose unit group has maximal cyclic component of size $2^{m-2}$
  - name: p
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p \ge 3$
    purpose: supplies the odd prime-power modulus $p^k$ and enables an LTE-based order computation for $1+p$
  - name: k
    type: int
    domain: integer $k \ge 3$
    purpose: sets the odd prime-power depth and forces the exponent kernel $p^{k-1}$
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $q \ne p,\ell$ with $q \equiv 1 \pmod 4$
    purpose: supplies the prime-square modulus $q^2$ and allows a Hensel-lifted square root of $-1$
  - name: ell
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $\ell \ne p,q$
    purpose: supplies an auxiliary prime modulus creating a fourth exponent congruence modulo $\ell-1$
  - name: u
    type: int
    domain: odd integer with $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(u)=2^{m-2}$
    purpose: makes the 2-adic power constraint translate to the linear congruence $n \equiv t \pmod{2^{m-2}}$
  - name: g
    type: int
    domain: integer $g$ with $\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(g)=q(q-1)$
    purpose: rigidifies exponents modulo $q^2$ and enables order-$4$ target classification
  - name: h
    type: int
    domain: integer $h$ with $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell}(h)=\ell-1$
    purpose: rigidifies exponents modulo $\ell$
  - name: t
    type: int
    domain: integer $0 \le t < 2^{m-2}$
    purpose: target exponent for the 2-adic constraint $A^n \equiv u^t \pmod{2^m}$
  - name: x0
    type: int
    domain: integer root of $x^2 \equiv -1 \pmod q$ with $q \nmid x_0$
    purpose: selects which of the two order-$4$ lifts is used in the $q^2$ constraint
  invariant(s):
  - name: CRT_synthesized_unit
    depends_on:
    - m
    - p
    - k
    - q
    - ell
    statement: $M=2^m p^k q^2 \ell$ has pairwise coprime factors, so CRT gives a unique $A \pmod M$ from the four local residues.
  - name: LTE_order_of_1_plus_p
    depends_on:
    - p
    - k
    statement: For odd prime $p$, $v_p((1+p)^n-1)=1+v_p(n)$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(1+p)=p^{k-1}$.
  - name: two_adic_max_order_invariant
    depends_on:
    - m
    - u
    statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(u)=2^{m-2}$, then $u^n \equiv u^t \pmod{2^m}$ iff $n \equiv t \pmod{2^{m-2}}$.
  - name: hensel_quartic_lift_invariant
    depends_on:
    - q
    - x0
    statement: If $x_0^2 \equiv -1 \pmod q$ and $q \nmid x_0$, then there is a unique $B \pmod{q^2}$ with $B \equiv x_0 \pmod q$ and $B^2 \equiv -1 \pmod{q^2}$; this $B$ has order $4$ modulo $q^2$.
  - name: coupling_gcd_compatibility
    depends_on:
    - m
    - q
    - ell
    - t
    statement: The exponent system requires compatibility modulo shared gcds when combining $2^{m-2}$, $q(q-1)$, and $\ell-1$ via gcd reduction.
  operator(s):
  - Chinese_remainder
  - modular_reduction
  - LTE_valuation
  - multiplicative_order
  - order_translation
  - two_adic_LTE
  - Hensel_lifting
  - quadratic_reciprocity
  - primitive_root_mod_prime_power
  - primitive_root_usage
  - cyclic_exponent_identification
  - gcd_based_reduction
  - linear_congruence_solvability
  - generalized_CRT_sequential
  - modular_exponentiation
  essential_assumption(s):
  - $u$ must have maximal order $2^{m-2}$ modulo $2^m$ so the 2-adic condition becomes a single linear congruence in $n$.
  - $q \equiv 1 \pmod 4$ and $q \nmid x_0$ are required so $x^2 \equiv -1 \pmod q$ has a simple root that lifts uniquely to $q^2$.
  - $g$ being a primitive root modulo $q^2$ is required to avoid discrete logarithm ambiguity and to classify the order-$4$ target exponent uniquely once $x_0$ is fixed.
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - The four power congruences translate to the coupled exponent system $n \equiv t \pmod{2^{m-2}}$, $n \equiv 0 \pmod{p^{k-1}}$, $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$, $n \equiv (\ell-1)/2 \pmod{\ell-1}$, where $r$ is uniquely forced by the chosen Hensel lift (pinned by $x_0$). Solving by gcd reduction and sequential CRT yields a single residue class, hence a unique least positive $n_0$ and a unique $N \equiv n_0 \pmod{999}$ with $0 \le N < 999$.
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: Chinese_remainder
    inputs:
    - congruences modulo $2^m$, $p^k$, $q^2$, $\ell$
    purpose: construct the unique CRT-synthesized unit $A$ modulo $2^m p^k q^2 \ell$
    outputs:
    - unique $A \pmod M$
  - name: LTE_valuation
    inputs:
    - $(1+p)^n-1$ at odd prime $p$
    purpose: compute $v_p((1+p)^n-1)$ and force $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(1+p)=p^{k-1}$
    outputs:
    - exponent kernel $p^{k-1}\mid n$
  - name: two_adic_LTE
    inputs:
    - $u^{2^t}-1$ for odd $u$
    purpose: compute 2-adic valuations and orders modulo $2^m$
    outputs:
    - determine $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(u)$ and translate $u^n \equiv u^t$ into $n \equiv t \pmod{2^{m-2}}$
  - name: quadratic_reciprocity
    inputs:
    - Legendre symbol $\left(\frac{-1}{q}\right)$
    purpose: guarantee solvability of $x^2 \equiv -1 \pmod q$ when $q \equiv 1 \pmod 4$
    outputs:
    - existence of $x_0$ needed for Hensel lifting
  - name: Hensel_lifting
    inputs:
    - $x_0^2 \equiv -1 \pmod q$ with $q \nmid x_0$
    purpose: lift $x_0$ uniquely to $B \pmod{q^2}$ solving $B^2 \equiv -1 \pmod{q^2}$
    outputs:
    - unique $B$ modulo $q^2$
  - name: primitive_root_mod_prime_power
    inputs:
    - primitive root $g$ modulo $q^2$
    purpose: rigidify exponents modulo $q^2$ and classify order-$4$ elements
    outputs:
    - convert $A^n \equiv B \pmod{q^2}$ into $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$
  - name: primitive_root_usage
    inputs:
    - primitive root $h$ modulo $\ell$
    purpose: translate $A^n \equiv h^{(\ell-1)/2}$ into an exponent congruence modulo $\ell-1$
    outputs:
    - $n \equiv (\ell-1)/2 \pmod{\ell-1}$
  - name: gcd_based_reduction
    inputs:
    - non-coprime exponent moduli
    purpose: reduce and solve intermediate linear congruences by dividing by gcd and inverting
    outputs:
    - arithmetic progression for $n$
  - name: generalized_CRT_sequential
    inputs:
    - progression for $n$ and remaining exponent congruences
    purpose: sequentially incorporate constraints to reach a single residue class
    outputs:
    - least positive $n_0$
  - name: modular_exponentiation
    inputs:
    - large integer $n_0$
    purpose: compute $n_0 \bmod 999$
    outputs:
    - normalized answer $N$
  theorem(s):
  - name: Chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
    role: existence and uniqueness of $A$ modulo $2^m p^k q^2 \ell$
  - name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE_odd_prime
    role: valuation formula $v_p((1+p)^n-1)=v_p(1+p-1)+v_p(n)$ for odd prime $p$
  - name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE_p_equals_2
    role: valuation formula $v_2(a^n-b^n)=v_2(a-b)+v_2(a+b)+v_2(n)-1$ for odd $a,b$ and even $n$
  - name: quadratic_reciprocity_supplement_minus_one
    role: criterion $\left(\frac{-1}{q}\right)=(-1)^{(q-1)/2}$
  - name: Hensel_lemma_simple_root
    role: unique lifting of a simple root modulo $q$ to modulo $q^2$
  - name: Euler_totient_prime_power_formula
    role: $\varphi(q^2)=q(q-1)$
  - name: cyclic_group_order_formula
    role: order of $g^a$ in a cyclic group is $m/\gcd(m,a)$
  - name: linear_congruence_theorem
    role: solvability and reduction of $ax \equiv b \pmod m$
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - Equality of powers in a finite cyclic group is equivalent to congruence of exponents modulo the group order.
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - Convert each power condition on $A^n$ into a linear congruence on $n$ using LTE, 2-adic LTE, primitive-root rigidity, and an order-$4$ Hensel-lifted target.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: CRT__prime_power_times_prime_square_times_prime__three_exponent_targets

problem_spec:
  summary: Build $A$ by CRT modulo $p^k q^2 \ell$ with $A \equiv 1+p \pmod{p^k}$, $A \equiv g \pmod{q^2}$, and $A \equiv h \pmod{\ell}$. Convert the three conditions $A^n \equiv 1 \pmod{p^k}$, $A^n \equiv g^r \pmod{q^2}$, $A^n \equiv h^s \pmod{\ell}$ into a coupled exponent system and solve it by gcd reduction and modular inversion.
  object(s):
  - name: p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: k
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent

    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: g
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: h
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: r
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: s
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: B
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: C
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent

    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent

    dependency: dependent

    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  - name: N
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - $p,q,\ell$ are pairwise distinct odd primes with $q>3$.
  - $k$ is an integer with $k \ge 3$.
  - $g$ is a primitive root modulo $q^2$, so $\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(g)=\varphi(q^2)=q(q-1)$.
  - $h$ is a primitive root modulo $\ell$, so $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell}(h)=\ell-1$.
  - $r$ is an integer with $0<r<q(q-1)$ and $\gcd(p^{k-1},q(q-1)) \mid r$.
  - $s$ is an integer with $0<s<\ell-1$ such that the combined exponent system is consistent.
  - $B$ is the least nonnegative integer with $B \equiv g^r \pmod{q^2}$.
  - $C$ is the least nonnegative integer with $C \equiv h^s \pmod{\ell}$.
  - $M:=p^k q^2 \ell$.
  - $A$ is the least nonnegative integer with $A \equiv 1+p \pmod{p^k}$, $A \equiv g \pmod{q^2}$, and $A \equiv h \pmod{\ell}$.
  ask:
  - Determine the unique integer $N$ with $0 \le N < 1000$ and $N \equiv n_0 \pmod{1000}$, where $n_0$ is the least positive integer $n$ such that $A^n \equiv 1 \pmod{p^k}$, $A^n \equiv B \pmod{q^2}$, and $A^n \equiv C \pmod{\ell}$.
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p \ge 3$
    purpose: supplies the prime-power modulus $p^k$ and the LTE order of $1+p$ modulo $p^k$
  - name: k
    type: int
    domain: integer $k \ge 3$
    purpose: forces a nontrivial prime power modulus and yields the exponent kernel $p^{k-1}$
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $q \ne p$ with $q>3$
    purpose: supplies the prime square modulus $q^2$ with unit group order $q(q-1)$
  - name: ell
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $\ell$ distinct from $p$ and $q$
    purpose: adds an auxiliary prime modulus enforcing a third exponent congruence modulo $\ell-1$
  - name: g
    type: int
    domain: integer $g$ with $\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(g)=q(q-1)$
    purpose: rigidifies exponents modulo $q^2$ by turning $A^n \equiv g^r$ into $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$
  - name: h
    type: int
    domain: integer $h$ with $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell}(h)=\ell-1$
    purpose: rigidifies exponents modulo $\ell$ by turning $A^n \equiv h^s$ into $n \equiv s \pmod{\ell-1}$
  - name: r
    type: int
    domain: integer $r$ with $0<r<q(q-1)$ and $\gcd(p^{k-1},q(q-1)) \mid r$
    purpose: target exponent for the $q^2$ constraint and solvability guard against the $p^{k-1}$ kernel
  - name: s
    type: int
    domain: integer $s$ with $0<s<\ell-1$ consistent with the combined system
    purpose: target exponent for the auxiliary prime constraint
  invariant(s):
  - name: CRT_synthesized_unit
    depends_on:
    - p
    - k
    - q
    - ell
    statement: $M=p^k q^2 \ell$ with pairwise coprime factors, so CRT gives a unique $A \pmod M$ from local residues.
  - name: LTE_order_of_1_plus_p
    depends_on:
    - p
    - k
    statement: For odd prime $p$, $v_p((1+p)^n-1)=1+v_p(n)$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(1+p)=p^{k-1}$.
  - name: prime_square_generator_rigidity
    depends_on:
    - q
    - g
    statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(g)=q(q-1)$, then $g^n \equiv g^r \pmod{q^2}$ if and only if $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$.
  - name: auxiliary_prime_generator_rigidity
    depends_on:
    - ell
    - h
    statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell}(h)=\ell-1$, then $h^n \equiv h^s \pmod{\ell}$ if and only if $n \equiv s \pmod{\ell-1}$.
  operator(s):
  - Chinese_remainder
  - modular_reduction
  - LTE_valuation
  - multiplicative_order
  - order_translation
  - primitive_root_mod_prime_power
  - primitive_root_usage
  - cyclic_exponent_identification
  - gcd_based_reduction
  - linear_congruence_solvability
  - generalized_CRT_sequential
  - modular_exponentiation
  essential_assumption(s):
  - The condition that $g$ is a primitive root modulo $q^2$ is required to avoid discrete logarithm ambiguity modulo $q^2$.
  - The divisibility condition $\gcd(p^{k-1},q(q-1)) \mid r$ is required for solvability of $n \equiv 0 \pmod{p^{k-1}}$ and $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$.
  - The choice of $s$ must be consistent with the arithmetic progression forced by the first two congruences.
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - The three congruences on $A^n$ translate to the exponent system $n \equiv 0 \pmod{p^{k-1}}$, $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$, $n \equiv s \pmod{\ell-1}$. The first two determine a unique arithmetic progression by gcd reduction, and the third becomes a linear congruence in the progression parameter. This yields exactly one arithmetic progression of solutions, hence a unique least positive $n_0$ and a unique $N \equiv n_0 \pmod{1000}$ with $0 \le N < 1000$.
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: Chinese_remainder
    inputs:
    - congruences modulo $p^k$, $q^2$, and $\ell$
    purpose: construct $A$ modulo $p^k q^2 \ell$ from local residues
    outputs:
    - unique $A \pmod M$
  - name: LTE_valuation
    inputs:
    - $(1+p)^n-1$ at the prime $p$
    purpose: compute $v_p((1+p)^n-1)$ and force the exponent kernel $p^{k-1}$
    outputs:
    - $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(1+p)=p^{k-1}$
  - name: multiplicative_order
    inputs:
    - unit conditions modulo $p^k$, $q^2$, $\ell$
    purpose: translate power congruences into divisibility constraints on exponents
    outputs:
    - exponent congruences modulo $p^{k-1}$, $q(q-1)$, $\ell-1$
  - name: primitive_root_mod_prime_power
    inputs:

    purpose: rigidify exponents modulo $q^2$ by using $\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(g)=\varphi(q^2)$
    outputs:
    - $A^n \equiv g^r \pmod{q^2}$ becomes $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$
  - name: primitive_root_usage
    inputs:
    - primitive root $h$ modulo $\ell$
    purpose: rigidify exponents modulo $\ell$ using $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell}(h)=\ell-1$
    outputs:
    - $A^n \equiv h^s \pmod{\ell}$ becomes $n \equiv s \pmod{\ell-1}$
  - name: gcd_based_reduction
    inputs:
    - noncoprime moduli $p^{k-1}$ and $q(q-1)$
    purpose: solve $p^{k-1}t \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$ by dividing by $\gcd$ and inverting
    outputs:
    - arithmetic progression for the first two constraints
  - name: generalized_CRT_sequential
    inputs:
    - arithmetic progression from the first two constraints and the third congruence modulo $\ell-1$
    purpose: reduce the third constraint to a linear congruence in the progression parameter
    outputs:
    - refined progression and least solution $n_0$
  - name: modular_exponentiation
    inputs:
    - large integers reduced modulo $1000$
    purpose: compute $n_0 \bmod 1000$ without expanding $n_0$
    outputs:
    - final normalized answer $N$
  theorem(s):
  - name: Chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
    role: existence and uniqueness of $A$ modulo $p^k q^2 \ell$
  - name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE
    role: valuation formula $v_p((1+p)^n-1)=1+v_p(n)$ for odd prime $p$
  - name: Euler_totient_prime_power_formula
    role: $\varphi(q^2)=q(q-1)$
  - name: cyclic_group_exponent_equivalence
    role: in a cyclic group of order $m$, $g^n=g^r$ if and only if $n \equiv r \pmod m$
  - name: primitive_root_mod_prime
    role: $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell}(h)=\ell-1$ for $h$ a primitive root modulo $\ell$
  - name: linear_congruence_theorem
    role: solvability criterion and reduction for $ax \equiv b \pmod m$
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - In a cyclic group generated by $g$ of order $m$, equality of powers is equivalent to exponent congruence modulo $m$.
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - Use LTE to compute $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(1+p)=p^{k-1}$.
  - Use primitive roots modulo $q^2$ and $\ell$ to convert power congruences into linear exponent congruences.
  - Solve the exponent system by gcd reduction and a final linear congruence in a progression parameter.
  finish_type: extremal_argument
computational_context:

  library(s):

  - name: sympy

  import_hint(s):
  - import math
  - import sympy as sp

  - from sympy.ntheory.residue_ntheory import is_primitive_root
  task(s):

source_short_name: CRT order forcing with a $2^m$ component and valuation-sum normalization
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: multiplicative_order__principal_units__safe_prime_QR__2power_component__mixed_valuations_mod1000
  instance_id: nt_03_gen3_instance_2026_02_25
  short_name: CRT order forcing with a $2^m$ component and valuation-sum normalization
  generation: 3
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - modular arithmetic
  - multiplicative orders
  - p-adic valuations
  - 2-adic valuations
  - quadratic residues
  topic_path:
  - modular_arithmetic
  - orders_and_cycles
  - orders_mod_prime_powers_safe_primes_and_2_powers
  tags:
  - Chinese_remainder_theorem
  - multiplicative_order
  - lcm_of_local_orders
  - principal_units
  - LTE
  - LTE_v2
  - quadratic_reciprocity
  - Euler_criterion
  - safe_prime
  - valuations
  - modular_reduction_mod_1000
problem_spec:
  summary: A unit $a$ is defined by CRT constraints modulo $2^m$, $p^k$, $q^3$, and a safe prime $r=2q+1$. The local orders are forced using odd-prime LTE for the principal-unit congruences, a 2-adic LTE argument for the $2^m$ component, and quadratic reciprocity plus Euler's criterion for the safe-prime component. The global order $t$ is the least common multiple of the local orders, and the output is the normalized residue modulo $1000$ of $V=\frac{t}{4}(v_2(a^t-1)+v_p(a^t-1)+v_q(a^t-1))$.
  object(s):
  - name: p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: r
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: h
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: m
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: n
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: a
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: dependent
  - name: u
    type: sequence
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: t
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: N
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - $p$ and $q$ are odd primes with $p\ne q$.
  - $q\equiv 1\pmod 4$ and $r=2q+1$ is prime with $r\ne p$.
  - Integers $k\ge 2$, $m\ge 4$, and $h$ with $1\le h<k$ are fixed.
  - $n=2^m p^k q^3 r$.
  - $a\in\mathbb Z$ satisfies $a\equiv 1+p^h\pmod{p^k}$, $a\equiv 1+q\pmod{q^3}$, $a\equiv 5\pmod{2^m}$, and $a\equiv 2\pmod r$.
  - For $j\ge 0$, $u_j\equiv a^j\pmod n$, and $t$ is the least positive integer with $u_{j+t}=u_j$ for all $j\ge 0$.
  ask:
  - Determine $N\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$ such that $N\equiv \frac{t}{4}(v_2(a^t-1)+v_p(a^t-1)+v_q(a^t-1))\pmod{1000}$.
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p\ge 3$
    purpose: prime-power modulus $p^k$ with principal-unit depth parameter $h$
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $q\ge 5$ with $q\equiv 1\pmod 4$ and $r=2q+1$ prime
    purpose: supplies the $q^3$ modulus and the safe prime $r$ with $r\equiv 3\pmod 8$
  - name: r
    type: int
    domain: prime $r=2q+1$
    purpose: safe-prime modulus that forces a full order via quadratic character of $2$
  - name: k
    type: int
    domain: integer $k\ge 2$
    purpose: depth of the principal-unit congruence modulo $p^k$
  - name: h
    type: int
    domain: integer with $1\le h<k$
    purpose: forces $v_p(a-1)=h$ and changes the local order to $p^{k-h}$
  - name: m
    type: int
    domain: integer $m\ge 4$
    purpose: introduces a $2^m$ component so that $4\mid t$ is forced by 2-adic order arguments
  - name: a
    type: int
    domain: integer satisfying the stated CRT congruences modulo $2^m$, $p^k$, $q^3$, and $r$
    purpose: simultaneously forces local orders at $2$, $p$, $q$, and $r$
  invariant(s):
  - name: CRT_solvability
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    - r
    - k
    - m
    statement: The moduli $2^m$, $p^k$, $q^3$, and $r$ are pairwise coprime, so the system defining $a$ is solvable by CRT.
  - name: unit_condition
    depends_on:
    - a
    - n
    statement: The congruences force $\gcd(a,n)=1$, so $t=\operatorname{ord}_n(a)$ is defined.
  - name: odd_prime_LTE_forces_prime_power_orders
    depends_on:
    - a
    - p
    - q
    - k
    - h
    statement: From $v_p(a-1)=h$ and $v_q(a-1)=1$, odd-prime LTE forces $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(a)=p^{k-h}$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{q^3}(a)=q^2$.
  - name: two_adic_component_forces_divisibility_by_4
    depends_on:
    - a
    - m
    statement: From $a\equiv 5\pmod{2^m}$ with $m\ge 4$, 2-adic LTE forces $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(a)=2^{m-2}$, hence $4\mid t$.
  - name: safe_prime_nonresidue_of_2
    depends_on:
    - q
    - r
    statement: Since $r\equiv 3\pmod 8$, we have $\bigl(\frac{2}{r}\bigr)=-1$.
  - name: global_period_is_lcm
    depends_on:
    - a
    - n
    statement: With pairwise coprime factors, $t=\operatorname{ord}_n(a)$ equals the least common multiple of the local orders.
  - name: valuations_at_t_forced
    depends_on:
    - a
    - t
    - p
    - q
    statement: Odd-prime LTE and 2-adic LTE force $v_p(a^t-1)=k$, $v_q(a^t-1)=3$, and $v_2(a^t-1)=m$ for every valid instance.
  operator(s):
  - Chinese_remainder_theorem
  - multiplicative_order
  - order_mod_n
  - lcm_of_local_orders
  - v_p_valuation
  - LTE
  - v2_valuation
  - LTE_v2
  - Legendre_symbol
  - quadratic_reciprocity
  - Euler_criterion
  - modular_reduction_mod_1000
  essential_assumption(s):
  - $q\equiv 1\pmod 4$ and $r=2q+1$ is prime, so $r\equiv 3\pmod 8$ and $\bigl(\frac{2}{r}\bigr)=-1$.
  - $m\ge 4$, so the $2^m$ component forces $4\mid t$ and makes $\frac{t}{4}$ integral.
  - $1\le h<k$, so $v_p(a-1)=h$ is fixed and the $p^k$ order is nontrivial.
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - The congruence system fixes $v_p(a-1)=h$, $v_q(a-1)=1$, the residue class $a\equiv 5\pmod{2^m}$, and $a\equiv 2\pmod r$. Odd-prime LTE forces $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(a)=p^{k-h}$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{q^3}(a)=q^2$. 2-adic LTE forces $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(a)=2^{m-2}$, hence $4\mid t$. Quadratic reciprocity plus Euler's criterion force $\operatorname{ord}_r(a)=r-1$. Therefore $t=\operatorname{ord}_n(a)$ is uniquely forced as the least common multiple of the local orders, and the same LTE tools force $v_2(a^t-1)$, $v_p(a^t-1)$, and $v_q(a^t-1)$, so $V$ and hence $N\in\{0,\dots,999\}$ are unique.
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
    inputs:
    - pairwise coprime moduli $2^m$, $p^k$, $q^3$, $r$ and specified residues
    purpose: guarantee existence of $a$ and allow reduction to local congruence classes
    outputs:
    - a valid unit $a$ modulo $n$
  - name: order_mod_n
    inputs:
    - coprime factorization of $n$
    purpose: reduce $\operatorname{ord}_n(a)$ to the least common multiple of local orders
    outputs:
    - $t=\operatorname{lcm}(\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(a),\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(a),\operatorname{ord}_{q^3}(a),\operatorname{ord}_r(a))$
  - name: LTE_v_p_valuation
    inputs:
    - odd primes dividing $a-1$
    purpose: compute $v_p(a^n-1)$ and $v_q(a^n-1)$ from $v_p(a-1)$, $v_q(a-1)$ and the prime-power content of $n$
    outputs:
    - forced odd-prime valuations and prime-power orders
  - name: LTE_v2_valuation
    inputs:
    - odd $a$ and even exponent
    purpose: compute $v_2(a^n-1)$ and control the $2^m$ order component
    outputs:
    - forced 2-adic valuation and a forced factor $4\mid t$
  - name: quadratic_reciprocity
    inputs:
    - prime $r\equiv 3\pmod 8$
    purpose: evaluate $\bigl(\frac{2}{r}\bigr)$
    outputs:
    - $\bigl(\frac{2}{r}\bigr)=-1$
  - name: Euler_criterion
    inputs:
    - residue $a\equiv 2\pmod r$
    purpose: deduce $a^{(r-1)/2}\equiv -1\pmod r$ and force $\operatorname{ord}_r(a)=r-1$
    outputs:
    - the safe-prime local order
  - name: modular_reduction
    inputs:
    - integer $V$ and modulus $1000$
    purpose: produce the unique normalized output $N\in\{0,\dots,999\}$
    outputs:
    - final answer
  theorem(s):
  - name: Chinese_Remainder_Theorem
    role: existence of $a$ satisfying simultaneous congruences
  - name: Lifting_the_Exponent_Lemma_LTE_odd_prime_case

source_short_name: CRT-count of units with forced local orders, Legendre filtering, and order lifting to a prime square
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: multiplicative_order__crt_hensel_legendre_orderlift__mixed_prime_powers_with_2adic_component
  instance_id: nt_04_gen4_2026-02-25_reviewed_nt_04_4
  short_name: CRT-count of units with forced local orders, Legendre filtering, and order lifting to a prime square
  generation: 4
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - modular arithmetic
  - multiplicative order
  - p-adic lifting methods
  - quadratic residues
  - mixed prime powers
  topic_path:
  - modular_arithmetic
  - chinese_remainder_theorem
  - multiplicative_order_and_prime_power_lifting
  tags:
  - chinese_remainder_theorem
  - lcm_of_orders
  - binomial_expansion_mod_prime_power
  - hensel_lifting
  - quadratic_residues
  - euler_criterion
  - order_lifting_prime_square
  - primitive_root_mod_prime_square
  - exact_order_counting
  - euler_totient
problem_spec:
  summary: 'Count residue classes x modulo a product of prime powers using CRT, where local congruence conditions force

    specific local multiplicative orders and a global order constraint forces an order-lift on a prime-square component.

    A Legendre-symbol constraint is converted to an exponent constraint by Euler''s criterion, and the remaining count is

    obtained by exact-order counting inside a cyclic unit group modulo an odd prime square (verified by an explicit generator).'
  object(s):
  - name: M
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: dependent
  - name: x
    type: integer
    role: unknown
    dependency: independent
  - name: S
    type: set
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: C
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: A
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - M=2^6 \cdot 3^4 \cdot 7^2 \cdot 19^2.
  - x is a residue class modulo M with gcd(x,M)=1.
  - 'Constraints: ord_M(x)=2736; x\equiv3 (mod 8); x\equiv1 (mod 27) but x\neq1 (mod 81); x^3\equiv1 (mod 49) but x\neq1 (mod 7); (x/19)=1 and x^3\neq1 (mod 19).'
  ask:
  - Let C be the number of such residue classes x (mod M). Output the unique A in {0,...,999} with A\equivC (mod 1000).
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: fixed_instance
    type: enum
    domain: '{(2^6,3^4,7^2,19^2)}'
    purpose: This reviewed Gen-4 artifact is a single concrete instance of the daughter family.
  invariant(s):
  - name: crt_decomposition
    depends_on:
    - M
    statement: 'Since 64,81,49,361 are pairwise coprime and M=64 \cdot 81 \cdot 49 \cdot 361, CRT gives a bijection between x (mod M) and its tuple of residues

      (x (mod 64), x (mod 81), x (mod 49), x (mod 361)).'
  - name: lcm_of_local_orders
    depends_on:
    - M
    - x
    statement: For gcd(x,M)=1, ord_M(x)=lcm(ord_64(x), ord_81(x), ord_49(x), ord_361(x)).
  - name: forced_orders_from_local_constraints
    depends_on:
    - x
    statement: 'The constraints x\equiv3 (mod 8), x\equiv1 (mod 27) but not (mod 81), and x^3\equiv1 (mod 49) but x\neq1 (mod 7) force

      ord_64(x)=16, ord_81(x)=3, ord_49(x)=3.'
  - name: legendre_to_order9_mod19
    depends_on:
    - x
    statement: Euler criterion turns (x/19)=1 into x^9\equiv1 (mod 19); together with x^3\neq1 (mod 19) this forces ord_19(x)=9.
  - name: global_order_forces_prime_square_lift
    depends_on:
    - x
    statement: 'Because ord_M(x)=2^4 \cdot 3^2 \cdot 19 and the other local orders are not divisible by 19, the 19-factor must come from ord_361(x),

      forcing 19 | ord_361(x). Combined with ord_19(x)=9, a kernel-exponent argument forces ord_361(x)=171.'
  - name: cyclic_units_mod_19_square
    depends_on: []
    statement: The unit group modulo 19^2 has order φ(19^2)=342 and is cyclic; in fact 2 has order 342 modulo 361, so it generates the group.
  operator(s):
  - modular_exponentiation
  - chinese_remainder_theorem
  - multiplicative_order
  - legendre_symbol
  - euler_totient_function
  essential_assumption(s):
  - CRT applies because the prime-power factors are pairwise coprime.
  - Legendre symbol is evaluated only for gcd(x,19)=1, guaranteed by gcd(x,M)=1.
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - 'Local constraints force fixed local orders and fixed local counts at 64,81,49.

    The Legendre constraint forces ord_19(x)=9, and the global order constraint forces 19|ord_361(x).

    Under ord_19=9, the only possible values of ord_361 are 9 or 171; the extra divisibility by 19 forces ord_361=171 uniquely.

    Exact-order counting in the cyclic group (Z/361Z)^× gives exactly φ(171)=108 such residues, and CRT gives a bijection between global

    solutions and the product of the local solution sets, forcing a unique total count C and hence a unique A\equivC (mod 1000).'
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: chinese_remainder_theorem
    inputs:
    - M=64 \cdot 81 \cdot 49 \cdot 361 with pairwise coprime factors
    purpose: Split the global constraints into local constraints and combine local choices bijectively.
    outputs:
    - Bijection between S and a Cartesian product of local solution sets
  - name: order_lcm_in_direct_product
    inputs:
    - CRT group isomorphism on unit groups
    purpose: Translate the global order constraint into an lcm constraint on local orders.
    outputs:
    - Forces divisibility constraints on the remaining component order
  - name: binomial_expansion_mod_prime_power
    inputs:
    - Residues of the form 1+27t modulo 81
    purpose: Show (1+27t)^3\equiv1 (mod 81) and force ord_81=3.
    outputs:
    - Local order 3 modulo 81
  - name: cyclic_group_root_count
    inputs:
    - Explicit cyclicity of (Z/7Z)^×
    purpose: Count cube roots of unity modulo 7.
    outputs:
    - Exactly 3 roots modulo 7
  - name: hensel_lifting
    inputs:
    - Simple roots of Y^3-1 modulo 7
    purpose: Lift each root uniquely to modulo 49 and preserve nontriviality.
    outputs:
    - Exactly 3 roots modulo 49; 2 after excluding the 1 mod 7 root
  - name: two_adic_exponent_computation
    inputs:
    - u=8k+3 modulo 64
    purpose: Compute u^8 modulo 64 uniformly and force ord_64(u)=16.
    outputs:
    - Local order 16 modulo 64
  - name: euler_criterion_legendre_filter
    inputs:
    - Legendre symbol (x/19)
    purpose: Convert a quadratic-residue condition to x^9\equiv1 (mod 19).
    outputs:
    - Forces ord_19(x)=9 together with x^3\neq1 (mod 19)
  - name: kernel_exponent_order_lifting
    inputs:
    - Reduction map (Z/361Z)^× -> (Z/19Z)^× with kernel of size 19
    purpose: Show that under ord_19=9, the only possible ord_361 are 9 or 171, and use the global constraint to force 171.
    outputs:
    - Forced order 171 modulo 361
  - name: exact_order_counting
    inputs:
    - Cyclic group (Z/361Z)^× of order 342
    purpose: Count elements of exact order 171.
    outputs:
    - Exactly φ(171)=108 choices for the 361-component
  theorem(s):
  - name: chinese_remainder_theorem
    role: Bijection of residues and decomposition of unit groups modulo coprime factors.
  - name: hensel_lemma_simple_root
    role: Unique lifting of simple roots from modulo 7 to modulo 49.
  - name: euler_criterion
    role: Characterizes the Legendre symbol by an exponent test modulo an odd prime.
  - name: order_divisibility_under_homomorphism
    role: The order of an element maps to a divisor under a group homomorphism; used for mod 19 vs mod 361.
  - name: totient_exact_order_count
    role: In a cyclic group, the number of elements of order d is φ(d).
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - 'Local-to-global structure: CRT plus lcm coupling of multiplicative orders.'
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - Force fixed local orders on 2-, 3-, and 7-power components, then use the global order to force an order lift at 19^2.
  - Convert (x/19)=1 to x^9\equiv1 (mod 19) via Euler criterion, then exclude x^3\equiv1 to get order 9.
  - Count exact order 171 elements in (Z/361Z)^× using cyclicity shown by an explicit generator.
  finish_type: counting_argument
computational_context:
  preferred_language: python
  library(s):
  - name: math
    reason: gcd computations and modular arithmetic.
  - name: itertools
    reason: Enumerate the Cartesian product of local solution sets (size 3456).
  - name: sympy
    reason: Compute multiplicative orders, Legendre symbols, and CRT combinations for independent verification.
  task(s):
  - name: local_set_enumeration
    reason: Enumerate S_64, S_81, S_49, and S_361 and confirm their sizes and local orders.
  - name: crt_product_verification
    reason: Combine all local choices via CRT (3456 combinations) and verify all defining conditions including ord_M(x)=2736.
well_posed: 'yes'
instance_snapshot:
  derived:
    M: 91699776
    C: 3456
  final_answer:
    normalization: Unique A in {0,...,999} with A\equivC (mod 1000).
    value: 456
    normalized: 456

taxonomy:

  subdomain(s):
