source_short_name: Mixed LTE count with an order-2 bridge and exact depths
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: LTE__mixed_plus_minus__order2_bridge__exact_depth
  instance_id: athena_forger_nt00__2026-02-24T055019Z__nonce_8f5e
  short_name: Mixed LTE count with an order-2 bridge and exact depths
  generation: 2
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - p-adic valuations
  - LTE and exponent lifting
  - multiplicative orders in finite groups
  - Hensel lifting (simple roots)
  topic_path:
  - p_adic_valuations
  - LTE
  - mixed_sum_and_difference
  tags:
  - LTE_difference_of_powers
  - LTE_sum_of_powers_odd_exponent
  - order2_bridge_to_negative_one
  - parity_gate
  - hensel_simple_root_lift
  - chinese_remainder_theorem
  - gymnasium_ready
  - material_novelty
problem_spec:
  summary: Two-prime counting problem where one prime uses a difference-of-powers valuation and the other uses a sum-of-powers valuation; order data forces the relevant base congruences and exact p-adic depths are enforced by excluding the unique Hensel lift at the prime-square layer.
  object(s):
  - name: p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: A
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: B
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: d_p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: d_q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: n
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: M
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: x
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: independent
  - name: S
    type: set
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: N
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes.
  - $A,B\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $A\ge 2$ and $B\ge 2$.
  - $d_p\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $1<d_p\mid(p-1)$ and $q\nmid d_p$.
  - $d_q\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $1<d_q$ and $2d_q\mid(q-1)$ and $p\nmid d_q$.
  - 'Parity guard: $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ is odd.'
  - Define $M=p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$ and $n=\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)\,p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$.
  ask:
  - Let $S\subset(\mathbb{Z}/M\mathbb{Z})^\times$ be the set of residue classes $x\pmod M$ such that $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$, $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$, and simultaneously $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=B$. Determine $|S|$ and output the unique integer $0\le N<1000$ with $N\equiv |S|\pmod{1000}$.
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p\ge 3$
    purpose: prime at which a difference-of-powers LTE constraint is imposed
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $q\ge 3$ with $q\ne p$
    purpose: prime at which a sum-of-powers LTE constraint is imposed
  - name: A
    type: int
    domain: integer $A\ge 2$
    purpose: target exact depth $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and modulus exponent $p^{A+1}$
  - name: B
    type: int
    domain: integer $B\ge 2$
    purpose: target exact depth $v_q(x^n+1)=B$ and modulus exponent $q^{B+1}$
  - name: d_p
    type: int
    domain: integer $d_p$ with $1<d_p\mid(p-1)$ and $q\nmid d_p$
    purpose: prescribed nontrivial order modulo $p$; keeps $v_q(n/d_q)$ sharp
  - name: d_q
    type: int
    domain: integer $d_q$ with $1<d_q$ and $2d_q\mid(q-1)$ and $p\nmid d_q$
    purpose: half the prescribed even order modulo $q$ (order $2d_q$); keeps $v_p(n/d_p)$ sharp
  - name: n
    type: int
    domain: $n=\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$ (derived)
    purpose: structured exponent coupling the order constraints to exact valuation depths, and (via the parity guard) enabling LTE for a sum of powers
  - name: M
    type: int
    domain: $M=p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$ (derived)
    purpose: structured two-prime modulus for independent prime-power lifting and CRT recombination
  invariant(s):
  - name: order2_bridge_to_negative_one
    depends_on:
    - q
    - d_q
    statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ then $x^{d_q}$ has order $2$ in $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$ and hence $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$, so $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$.
  - name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
    depends_on:
    - d_p
    - d_q
    - n
    statement: The guard that $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ is odd implies $n/d_q$ is odd (since $p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$ is odd), enabling the odd-exponent LTE formula for $v_q(x^n+1)$.
  - name: structured_exponent_vp_targets
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    - A
    - B
    - d_p
    - d_q
    - n
    statement: Under $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$, one has $v_p(n/d_p)=A-1$ and $v_q(n/d_q)=B-1$.
  - name: mixed_order_to_LTE_bridge
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    - d_p
    - d_q
    - n
    statement: For admissible $x$, $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$ gives $p\mid(x^{d_p}-1)$ and yields (LTE) $v_p(x^n-1)=v_p(x^{d_p}-1)+v_p(n/d_p)$; and $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ gives $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$ and (with $n/d_q$ odd) yields $v_q(x^n+1)=v_q(x^{d_q}+1)+v_q(n/d_q)$.
  - name: exact_depth_equivalence
    depends_on:
    - A
    - B
    - d_p
    - d_q
    - n
    statement: Using the structured exponent valuations, the targets $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=B$ are equivalent to $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$.
  operator(s):
  - multiplicative_order_mod_prime
  - LTE_difference_of_powers
  - LTE_sum_of_powers_odd_exponent
  - compare_vp_on_both_sides
  - hensel_lift_simple_root
  - mod_prime_power_lifting_count
  - chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
  essential_assumption(s):
  - All primes are odd; no 2-adic exceptional LTE branches occur.
  - Cross-coprimality guards $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$ ensure $v_p(n/d_p)$ and $v_q(n/d_q)$ are exactly $A-1$ and $B-1$ with no extra prime factors leaking in from $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)$.
  - 'Parity guard: $n/d_q$ is odd (equivalently $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ odd), required for the sum-of-powers LTE identity at $q$.'
  - The order constraint at $q$ is even ($\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$) so that $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$; without this, $v_q(x^n+1)$ would not be controlled by LTE in the intended way.
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - For each admissible parameter tuple, the constraints reduce (via LTE bridges) to exact valuation conditions $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$. For each residue of order $d_p$ modulo $p$ and each residue of order $2d_q$ modulo $q$, Hensel's lemma for simple roots implies there is exactly one lift to the prime-square layer with valuation $\ge 2$ for the relevant polynomial ($y^{d_p}-1$ at $p$, $y^{d_q}+1$ at $q$), hence exactly $p-1$ (resp. $q-1$) admissible lifts with valuation $1$. All higher lifts from $r^2$ to $r^{A_r+1}$ are free and contribute a factor $r^{A_r-1}$. The $p$- and $q$-conditions are independent and recombine uniquely by CRT, so $|S|$ (hence $|S|\bmod 1000$) is uniquely determined.
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: order_count_in_cyclic_group_mod_prime
    inputs:
    - divisor conditions $d_p\mid(p-1)$ and $2d_q\mid(q-1)$
    purpose: Count residues of exact order $d_p$ modulo $p$ and exact order $2d_q$ modulo $q$ using cyclicity of unit groups modulo primes.
    outputs:
    - '$\#\{a\pmod p: \operatorname{ord}_p(a)=d_p\}=\varphi(d_p)$'
    - '$\#\{b\pmod q: \operatorname{ord}_q(b)=2d_q\}=\varphi(2d_q)$'
  - name: order2_to_negative_one_bridge
    inputs:
    - $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$
    purpose: Convert an even order condition into the congruence $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ needed for sum-of-powers LTE.
    outputs:
    - $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$ and hence $x^n+1=((x^{d_q})^{n/d_q}+1)$ is divisible by $q$
  - name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
    inputs:
    - the parity guard $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ odd
    purpose: Ensure $n/d_q$ is odd so the odd-exponent LTE sum formula applies.
    outputs:
    - $n/d_q$ odd
  - name: LTE_difference_of_powers
    inputs:
    - odd prime $p$ dividing $x^{d_p}-1$
    - exponent multiplier $n/d_p$
    purpose: Compute $v_p(x^n-1)$ exactly after order reduction to a difference of powers.
    outputs:
    - $v_p(x^n-1)=v_p(x^{d_p}-1)+v_p(n/d_p)$
  - name: LTE_sum_of_powers_odd_exponent
    inputs:
    - odd prime $q$ dividing $x^{d_q}+1$
    - odd exponent multiplier $n/d_q$
    purpose: Compute $v_q(x^n+1)$ exactly after order reduction to a sum of powers.
    outputs:
    - $v_q(x^n+1)=v_q(x^{d_q}+1)+v_q(n/d_q)$
  - name: structured_exponent_vp_targets
    inputs:
    - $n=\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$
    purpose: Extract the fixed values $v_p(n/d_p)=A-1$ and $v_q(n/d_q)=B-1$ under the cross-coprimality guards.
    outputs:
    - $v_p(n/d_p)=A-1$
    - $v_q(n/d_q)=B-1$
  - name: compare_vp_on_both_sides
    inputs:
    - exact valuation identities from LTE
    - explicit values of $v_p(n/d_p)$ and $v_q(n/d_q)$
    purpose: Turn target depths $A,B$ into prime-square exclusion statements on $x^{d_p}-1$ and $x^{d_q}+1$.
    outputs:
    - $v_p(x^n-1)=A \Longleftrightarrow v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$
    - $v_q(x^n+1)=B \Longleftrightarrow v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$
  - name: hensel_filter_for_exact_depth
    inputs:
    - polynomials $y^{d_p}-1$ and $y^{d_q}+1$ with simple roots modulo primes
    purpose: Show that among the $r$ lifts of a fixed root modulo $r$ to modulo $r^2$, exactly one lift makes the polynomial divisible by $r^2$; excluding it forces valuation exactly $1$.
    outputs:
    - exactly $p-1$ good lifts at the $p^2$ layer per order-$d_p$ residue
    - exactly $q-1$ good lifts at the $q^2$ layer per order-$2d_q$ residue
  - name: mod_prime_power_lifting_count
    inputs:
    - admissible classes modulo $r^2$
    - target prime power $r^{A_r+1}$
    purpose: Count free lifts from $r^2$ to $r^{A_r+1}$ and ensure the valuation decision persists under lifting.
    outputs:
    - multiplicative factor $r^{A_r-1}$ for each prime $r\in\{p,q\}$
  - name: CRT_product_count
    inputs:
    - independent counts modulo $p^{A+1}$ and $q^{B+1}$
    purpose: Recombine prime-power solutions into solutions modulo $M=p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$ via CRT bijection.
    outputs:
    - $|S|=N_p N_q$
  theorem(s):
  - name: cyclicity_of_units_mod_odd_prime
    role: $(\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z})^\times$ is cyclic; enables exact-order counting via Euler totient.
  - name: unique_element_of_order_2_in_cyclic_group
    role: A cyclic group has a unique element of order $2$; in $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$ it is $-1$, giving $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ when $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$.
  - name: vp_sum_lower_bound
    role: Valuation bookkeeping used inside LTE arguments to isolate the first nonvanishing term.
  - name: LTE_difference_of_powers_for_odd_prime
    role: If odd prime $r\mid(a-b)$ and $r\nmid ab$, then $v_r(a^m-b^m)=v_r(a-b)+v_r(m)$.
  - name: LTE_sum_of_powers_for_odd_prime_odd_exponent
    role: If odd prime $r\mid(a+b)$, $r\nmid ab$, and $m$ is odd, then $v_r(a^m+b^m)=v_r(a+b)+v_r(m)$.
  - name: hensel_unique_lift_for_simple_roots
    role: Simple roots of a polynomial modulo an odd prime lift uniquely to roots modulo the prime square; used for both $y^{d_p}-1$ and $y^{d_q}+1$.
  - name: chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
    role: Provides a bijection between residues modulo a product of coprime moduli and pairs of residues modulo each factor.
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - Translate multiplicative-order information into divisibility of $x^d\pm 1$, then use LTE (difference and sum) to obtain exact p-adic depths and count solutions by lifting across prime powers.
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - Use the even order condition $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ to force $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ (unique order-2 element) and combine with a parity gate so that LTE applies to the sum $x^n+1$.
  - Apply LTE separately to $x^n-1$ at $p$ and to $x^n+1$ at $q$ to convert target depths into the prime-square exclusions $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$.
  - Count by excluding the unique Hensel lift at each prime-square layer and then freely lifting to higher prime powers, and recombine counts via CRT.
  finish_type: counting_argument
computational_context:
  preferred_language: python
  library(s):
  - name: math
    reason: Integer arithmetic (lcm, modular exponentiation, gcd, valuation loops).
  - name: sympy
    reason: Canonicalization of kernel polynomials for the novelty equivalence audit.
  - name: random
    reason: Randomized stress tests comparing direct valuations with the LTE-predicted identities.
  task(s):
  - name: primary_structural_check
    reason: 'Correctness checks aligned to the intended structure:

      - Enumerated all residues modulo $11^2$ and counted those whose reduction mod $11$ has order $5$ and for which $11\mid(x^5-1)$ but $11^2\nmid(x^5-1)$; obtained $40$ and lifted by a factor $11^3$ to get $N_{11}=53240$.

      - Enumerated all residues modulo $19^2$ and counted those whose reduction mod $19$ has order $18$ and for which $19\mid(x^9+1)$ but $19^2\nmid(x^9+1)$; obtained $108$ and lifted by a factor $19^3$ to get $N_{19}=740772$.

      - Multiplied the independent prime-power counts (CRT interface) to get $|S|=39438701280$, hence $|S|\bmod 1000=280$.

      Preflight attempts:

      Attempt A: Naive heuristic (ignore exact-depth filters and only count order conditions with arbitrary lifts) gives $\varphi(5)\,11^4\cdot\varphi(18)\,19^4\equiv 264\pmod{1000}$, which differs from $280$.

      Attempt B: Bounded scan over $x\in\{1,2,\dots,200000\}$ viewed as distinct residues modulo $M$ found $19785$ hits, i.e. $785\bmod 1000$, not $280$.

      Attempt C: Partial information (e.g. only knowing $|S|\equiv 30\pmod{125}$) leaves $8$ candidates in $\{0,\dots,999\}$ (namely $30,155,280,405,530,655,780,905$), so it cannot determine the output without the full structure.'
  - name: secondary_independent_check
    reason: 'Independent verification via valuation identities:

      - "Random stress test on residues modulo $11^5$: for sampled $x$ satisfying the order-$5$ condition mod $11$, verified directly (by checking congruences mod $11^k$) that $v_{11}(x^n-1)=v_{11}(x^5-1)+v_{11}(n/5)$."

      - "Random stress test on residues modulo $19^5$: for sampled $x$ satisfying the order-$18$ condition mod $19$, verified directly that $v_{19}(x^n+1)=v_{19}(x^9+1)+v_{19}(n/9)$ (using the odd-exponent sum LTE regime)."

      - "Verified the \"unique bad lift property computationally: for each order-$5$ residue mod $11$, among its $11$ lifts to mod $11^2$ exactly one satisfies $y^5\\equiv 1\\pmod{11^2}$; for each order-$18$ residue mod $19$, among its $19$ lifts to mod $19^2$ exactly one satisfies $y^9\\equiv -1\\pmod{19^2}$."'
  - name: novelty_equivalence_audit
    reason: 'Canonicalized the parent and daughter kernel polynomials in sympy by expand+together+cancel+factor and monic normalization, and attempted equivalence under a deterministic symmetry set:

      - swap the two prime components (p<->q),

      - substitute $z\mapsto -z$,

      - substitute $z\mapsto 1/z$ with denominator clearing,

      - substitute $z\mapsto -1/z$ with denominator clearing.

      No transform made the parent kernel multiset match the daughter''s (the daughter includes a sum-of-powers constraint $z^{d_q}+1$ whereas the parent uses only $z^d-1$ constraints).'

source_short_name: Mixed LTE count with prime-square first digits and Legendre coupling
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: LTE__mixed_plus_minus__first_digit_legendre_coupling
  instance_id: nt_08_3__2026-02-25__v1
  short_name: Mixed LTE count with prime-square first digits and Legendre coupling
  generation: 3
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - p-adic valuations
  - LTE and exponent lifting
  - multiplicative orders modulo primes
  - prime-square lifting via binomial expansion
  - quadratic residues and Legendre symbol
  - Chinese remainder theorem
  topic_path:
  - p_adic_valuations
  - LTE
  - prime_square_digits_and_quadratic_characters
  tags:
  - LTE_difference_of_powers
  - LTE_sum_of_powers_odd_exponent
  - order2_bridge_to_negative_one
  - prime_square_first_digit
  - legendre_symbol_coupling
  - CRT_counting
problem_spec:
  summary: Two-prime counting problem with mixed valuation constraints $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=B$, reduced by LTE to prime-square exactness, then refined by coupling the Legendre signs of the prime-square first digits of $x^{d_p}-1$ and $x^{d_q}+1$.
  object(s):
  - name: p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: A
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: B
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: d_p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: d_q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: n
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: M
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: x
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: independent
  - name: a
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: b
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: S
    type: set
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: N
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes.
  - $A,B\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $A\ge 2$ and $B\ge 2$.
  - $d_p\mid(p-1)$ and $1<d_p$.
  - $2d_q\mid(q-1)$ and $1<d_q$.
  - Define $M=p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$ and $n=\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$.
  - Work in the unit group $(\mathbb{Z}/M\mathbb{Z})^\times$.
  ask:
  - Let $S\subset(\mathbb{Z}/M\mathbb{Z})^\times$ be the set of classes $x\pmod M$ with $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$, $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$, $v_p(x^n-1)=A$, $v_q(x^n+1)=B$, and with prime-square first digits defined by $x^{d_p}\equiv 1+pa\pmod{p^2}$ and $x^{d_q}\equiv -1+qb\pmod{q^2}$ satisfying $(\frac{a}{p})=(\frac{b}{q})$. Determine $|S|$ and output the unique $N$ with $0\le N<1000$ and $N\equiv |S|\pmod{1000}$.
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p\ge 3$
    purpose: prime for the difference-of-powers LTE constraint and the digit $a$ modulo $p$
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $q\ge 3$, $q\ne p$
    purpose: prime for the sum-of-powers LTE constraint and the digit $b$ modulo $q$
  - name: A
    type: int
    domain: integer $A\ge 2$
    purpose: target depth $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and modulus exponent $p^{A+1}$
  - name: B
    type: int
    domain: integer $B\ge 2$
    purpose: target depth $v_q(x^n+1)=B$ and modulus exponent $q^{B+1}$
  - name: d_p
    type: int
    domain: integer $d_p$ with $1<d_p\mid(p-1)$ and $q\nmid d_p$
    purpose: prescribed order modulo $p$, reducing $x^n-1$ to $(x^{d_p})^{n/d_p}-1$
  - name: d_q
    type: int
    domain: integer $d_q$ with $1<d_q$ and $2d_q\mid(q-1)$ and $p\nmid d_q$
    purpose: half the prescribed even order modulo $q$, forcing $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$
  - name: n
    type: int
    domain: $n=\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$
    purpose: structured exponent linking order data to valuation targets
  - name: M
    type: int
    domain: $M=p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$
    purpose: modulus enabling independent prime-power analysis and CRT recombination
  invariant(s):
  - name: order2_bridge_to_negative_one
    depends_on:
    - q
    - d_q
    statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ then $x^{d_q}$ has order $2$ modulo $q$, hence $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ and $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$.
  - name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
    depends_on:
    - d_p
    - d_q
    - n
    statement: The condition $n/d_q$ is odd is required for the odd-exponent LTE formula for $v_q(x^n+1)$.
  - name: LTE_depth_reduction
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    - d_p
    - d_q
    - n
    statement: Under the order constraints and parity gate, $v_p(x^n-1)=v_p(x^{d_p}-1)+v_p(n/d_p)$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=v_q(x^{d_q}+1)+v_q(n/d_q)$.
  - name: prime_square_first_digit
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    - d_p
    - d_q
    statement: If $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ then there is a unique $a\in(\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z})^\times$ with $x^{d_p}\equiv 1+pa\pmod{p^2}$, and if $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$ then there is a unique $b\in(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$ with $x^{d_q}\equiv -1+qb\pmod{q^2}$.
  - name: affine_first_digit_bijection
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    statement: For fixed $y_0$ modulo an odd prime $r$ with $y_0^m\equiv \pm 1\pmod r$ and $r\nmid m$, the lifts $y_0+rk$ modulo $r^2$ make the digit $(y^m\mp 1)/r$ run through all classes modulo $r$ bijectively.
  - name: quadratic_character_balance
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    statement: Exactly half of $(\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z})^\times$ are quadratic residues for an odd prime $r$.
  - name: CRT_sign_coupling
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    statement: CRT gives a bijection between residues modulo $p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$ and pairs modulo $p^{A+1}$ and $q^{B+1}$, and the only cross-condition is $(\frac{a}{p})=(\frac{b}{q})$.
  operator(s):
  - multiplicative_order_mod_prime
  - p_adic_valuation
  - LTE_difference_of_powers
  - LTE_sum_of_powers_odd_exponent
  - binomial_expansion_mod_prime_square
  - legendre_symbol
  - chinese_remainder_theorem
  essential_assumption(s):
  - $p$ and $q$ are odd primes, so LTE applies in the standard odd-prime forms.
  - $n/d_q$ is odd so the sum-LTE formula applies at $q$.
  - $p\nmid d_p$ and $q\nmid d_q$, so the first-digit map has nonzero slope modulo the prime.
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - The LTE identities force the prime-square exactness conditions $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$. Lemma 1 makes the first digits $a$ and $b$ uniformly distributed among nonzero residues when lifting from modulo a prime to modulo the prime square, and Lemma 2 forces an exact half split by Legendre sign. Higher lifts preserve these first digits. CRT recombines the local counts, and the sign-coupling constraint selects a unique total count, giving a unique $N$ modulo $1000$.
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: order_count_in_cyclic_group_mod_prime
    inputs:
    - $d_p\mid(p-1)$
    - $2d_q\mid(q-1)$
    purpose: Count residues of exact orders $d_p$ and $2d_q$ modulo $p$ and $q$.
    outputs:
    - $\varphi(d_p)$ and $\varphi(2d_q)$ counts at the prime layer
  - name: order2_to_negative_one_bridge
    inputs:
    - $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$
    purpose: Force $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ to start the sum-of-powers valuation chain.
    outputs:
    - $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$
  - name: LTE_difference_of_powers
    inputs:
    - $p\mid(x^{d_p}-1)$
    - $p\nmid x$
    purpose: Relate $v_p(x^n-1)$ to $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)$ and $v_p(n/d_p)$.
    outputs:
    - $v_p(x^n-1)=v_p(x^{d_p}-1)+v_p(n/d_p)$
  - name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
    inputs:
    - $n/d_q$
    purpose: Check that $n/d_q$ is odd so LTE for $a^m+b^m$ applies.
    outputs:
    - $n/d_q$ odd
  - name: LTE_sum_of_powers_odd_exponent
    inputs:
    - $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$
    - odd exponent $n/d_q$
    purpose: Relate $v_q(x^n+1)$ to $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)$ and $v_q(n/d_q)$.
    outputs:
    - $v_q(x^n+1)=v_q(x^{d_q}+1)+v_q(n/d_q)$
  - name: first_digit_affine_bijection_mod_prime_square
    inputs:
    - lifts $y=y_0+rk$
    purpose: Show first digits modulo $r$ run bijectively, with exactly one excluded lift of higher depth.
    outputs:
    - uniform distribution of $a$ and $b$ in $(\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z})^\times$
  - name: quadratic_residue_balance
    inputs:
    - odd prime $r$
    purpose: Split $(\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z})^\times$ evenly by Legendre sign.
    outputs:
    - exactly $(r-1)/2$ residues of each sign
  - name: CRT_coupled_count
    inputs:
    - signed local counts at $p$ and $q$
    purpose: Combine local counts under the sign coupling using CRT.
    outputs:
    - $|S|$ and $|S|\bmod 1000$
  theorem(s):
  - name: cyclicity_of_units_mod_odd_prime
    role: $(\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z})^\times$ is cyclic; enables exact-order counting.
  - name: unique_element_of_order_2_in_cyclic_group
    role: In $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$, the unique element of order $2$ is $-1$.
  - name: LTE_difference_of_powers_for_odd_prime
    role: If odd prime $r\mid(a-b)$ and $r\nmid ab$ then $v_r(a^m-b^m)=v_r(a-b)+v_r(m)$.
  - name: LTE_sum_of_powers_for_odd_prime_odd_exponent
    role: If odd prime $r\mid(a+b)$, $r\nmid ab$, and $m$ is odd then $v_r(a^m+b^m)=v_r(a+b)+v_r(m)$.
  - name: binomial_expansion_mod_prime_square
    role: $(y_0+rk)^m\equiv y_0^m+m y_0^{m-1}rk\pmod{r^2}$; gives the affine digit map.
  - name: count_quadratic_residues_mod_prime
    role: Exactly $(r-1)/2$ nonzero residues are quadratic residues modulo an odd prime $r$.
  - name: chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
    role: CRT gives a bijection for coprime moduli and enables product counting with a coupling filter.
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - 'Use local-to-global structure: reduce global congruence and valuation conditions to independent prime-power constraints, then recombine via CRT with a character-level coupling invariant.'
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - 'Use mixed LTE: difference at $p$ and sum at $q$ unlocked by an even-order bridge and an odd-exponent parity gate.'
  - Introduce prime-square first digits and force a Legendre-sign coupling, turning the count into a signed CRT product.
  finish_type: counting_argument
computational_context:
  preferred_language: python
  library(s):
  - name: math
    reason: $\gcd$, $\operatorname{lcm}$, modular exponentiation, integer arithmetic.
  - name: itertools
    reason: Enumerate units modulo $p^2$ and $q^2$ to verify local signed counts.
  import_hint(s):
  - import math
  - import itertools
  task(s):
  - name: local_prime_square_enumeration
    reason: Enumerate all units modulo $13^2$ and $31^2$, filter by order conditions modulo $13$ and $31$, check prime-square exactness for $x^3-1$ and $x^{15}+1$, compute the digits $a$ and $b$, compute Legendre symbols by Euler's criterion, and tally signed counts. Multiply by lift factors $13^2$ and $31^2$ and apply the CRT sign coupling to recover $|S|$.
well_posed: 'yes'

    q: 31

    B: 3
    d_p: 3
    d_q: 15
    n: 2436135
    M: 26376683281
  final_answer:
    value: 467737920
    normalized: 920

source_short_name: Mobius inversion on a triple-prime modulus with dual valuation extraction, 2-adic LTE order control, prime-power order lifting, and Hensel square-root counting
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: Dirichlet_convolution__mobius_inversion__triple_twist__dual_valuation_unit__2adic_LTE__order_lift__hensel_roots__CRT_shared_config
  instance_id: athena:number_theory_11:2026-02-25:inst_gen4_triple_twist_dual_valuation_order_plus_rootcount_nt_11_4
  short_name: Mobius inversion on a triple-prime modulus with dual valuation extraction, 2-adic LTE order control, prime-power order lifting, and Hensel square-root counting
  generation: 4
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - arithmetic functions
  - Mobius inversion
  - p-adic valuation
  - 2-adic analysis
  - lifting the exponent
  - multiplicative order
  - Chinese remainder theorem
  - quadratic residues
  - Hensel lifting
  topic_path:
  - arithmetic_functions
  - mobius_inversion
  - prime_powers
  - unit_group_orders
  - two_adic_LTE
  - quadratic_residues
  - hensel_lifting
  - CRT_recombination
  tags:
  - Dirichlet_convolution_optional
  - mobius_inversion_optional
  - mu_mobius_optional
  - v_p_valuation
  - lifting_the_exponent_LTE
  - two_adic_LTE
  - order_lifting_prime_powers
  - multiplicative_order

  - lcm_operator
  - Legendre_symbol
  - quadratic_reciprocity
  - Hensel_lemma
  - CRT_solution_counting
  - shared_config_composition
problem_spec:
  summary: 'On the divisor lattice of $n_0=2^a p^k q^2$, define a valuation-sensitive function $F$ with three additive twists: one active on multiples of $2$, one on multiples of $p$, and one on multiples of $q$. Recover $f(n_0)$ uniquely by Möbius inversion, extract both $t_2=v_2(f(n_0))$ and $t_p=v_p(f(n_0))$ to form the unit $M=f(n_0)/(2^{t_2}p^{t_p})$. Then compute (i) $r=\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)$ using $2$-adic LTE for the $2^a$ component, LTE-based order lifting for odd prime powers, and CRT-lcm recombination, and (ii) the root count $s$ for $x^2\equiv M$ on the odd part using quadratic reciprocity, Hensel lifting, and CRT counting. Output $r+s$.'
  object(s):
  - name: p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: a
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: k
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: A
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: B
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: C
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: n_0
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: dependent
  - name: F
    type: function
    role: given
    dependency: dependent
  - name: f
    type: function
    role: unknown
    dependency: dependent
  - name: t_2
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: t_p
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: M
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: r
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: s
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: out
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes; $a\ge 3$ and $k\ge 2$
  - $n_0=2^a p^k q^2$
  - $A,B,C$ are integers with $\gcd(A,q)=1$ and $\gcd(B,p)=1$
  - For each divisor $n\mid n_0$ with $u=v_2(n)$, $i=v_p(n)$, $j=v_q(n)$, define $F(n)=n+C\mathbf{1}_{u\ge 1}\cdot n/2 + B\mathbf{1}_{i\ge 1}\cdot n/p + A\mathbf{1}_{j\ge 1}\cdot n/q$
  - There exists an arithmetic function $f$ on divisors of $n_0$ such that $F(n)=\sum_{d\mid n} f(d)$ for all $n\mid n_0$
  - Define $t_2=v_2(f(n_0))$, $t_p=v_p(f(n_0))$, and $M=f(n_0)/(2^{t_2}p^{t_p})$
  - Let $r=\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)$ and $s=\#\{x\bmod (p^k q^2):x^2\equiv M \pmod{p^k q^2}\}$
  ask:
  - Compute $f(n_0)$ and determine $t_2$, $t_p$, and $M$
  - Determine $r=\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)$ and $s$, then output $r+s$
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: none
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p
    type: int
    domain: odd prime
    purpose: base odd prime for the $p^k$ component and $p$-adic lifting
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime with $q\ne p$
    purpose: base odd prime for the $q^2$ component and residue/order computations
  - name: a
    type: int
    domain: integer with $a\ge 3$
    purpose: depth of the $2$-power modulus $2^a$ introducing a 2-adic component in CRT
  - name: k
    type: int
    domain: integer with $k\ge 2$
    purpose: depth of the prime-power modulus $p^k$
  - name: A
    type: int
    domain: integer with $\gcd(A,q)=1$
    purpose: $q$-side twist coefficient in $F$
  - name: B
    type: int
    domain: integer with $\gcd(B,p)=1$
    purpose: $p$-side twist coefficient in $F$
  - name: C
    type: int
    domain: integer
    purpose: $2$-side twist coefficient in $F$
  invariant(s):
  - name: convolution_encoding
    depends_on:
    - n_0
    statement: $F(n)=\sum_{d\mid n} f(d)$ is equivalent to $F=f*\mathbf{1}$ under Dirichlet convolution on divisors of $n_0$
  - name: mobius_inverse_of_1
    depends_on:
    - n_0
    statement: $\mu*\mathbf{1}=\varepsilon$ on the divisor lattice, so $f$ is uniquely determined by $f=F*\mu$
  - name: squarefree_reduction_for_2a_p_k_q2
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    - a
    - k
    statement: For $n_0=2^a p^k q^2$, Möbius inversion collapses to the 8-term inclusion-exclusion over squarefree $d\mid 2pq$
  - name: full_support_evaluation
    depends_on:
    - a
    - k
    statement: If $a\ge 3$ and $k\ge 2$ (and $q^2$ fixed), every $n_0/d$ for squarefree $d\mid 2pq$ remains divisible by $2,p,q$, so the full three-twist formula for $F(n_0/d)$ applies uniformly
  - name: closed_form_f_n0
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    - A
    - B
    - C
    statement: Uniform full-support implies $f(n_0)=n_0\prod_{\ell\in\{2,p,q\}}(1-\ell^{-1})\cdot(1+C/2+B/p+A/q)$
  - name: dual_valuation_unit_extraction
    depends_on:
    - n_0
    statement: Defining $M=f(n_0)/(2^{v_2(f(n_0))}p^{v_p(f(n_0))})$ forces $\gcd(M,2p)=1$; combined with a mod-$q$ check yields $\gcd(M,n_0)=1$
  - name: two_adic_LTE_growth
    depends_on:
    - a
    statement: For odd $M$, $v_2(M^{2^m}-1)=v_2(M-1)+v_2(M+1)+m-1$ controls $\operatorname{ord}_{2^a}(M)$
  - name: odd_prime_order_lifting
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    statement: LTE-based valuation growth yields exact order lifting from $\operatorname{ord}_\ell(M)$ to $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell^e}(M)$ for odd primes $\ell$
  - name: quadratic_residue_hensel_invariant
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    statement: If $\left(\frac{M}{\ell}\right)=1$ for an odd prime $\ell$, then $x^2\equiv M\pmod{\ell^e}$ has exactly 2 solutions for all $e\ge 1$ by Hensel lifting
  - name: CRT_coupling_shared_config
    depends_on:
    - n_0
    statement: 'The same extracted unit $M$ drives two coupled outputs: $r=\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)$ (via CRT-lcm) and $s$ (via CRT-product of root counts), and the asked quantity is $r+s$'
  operator(s):
  - Dirichlet_convolution_optional
  - mobius_inversion_optional
  - mu_mobius_optional
  - v_p_valuation
  - lifting_the_exponent_LTE
  - two_adic_LTE
  - order_lifting_prime_powers
  - multiplicative_order
  - Chinese_remainder_theorem
  - Legendre_symbol
  - quadratic_reciprocity
  - Hensel_lifting
  - lcm_operator
  essential_assumption(s):
  - 'Möbius inversion applies: $F=f*\mathbf{1}$ implies $f=F*\mu$ on divisors of $n_0$'
  - The extracted $M$ is coprime to $n_0$, so $\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)$ is defined
  - Odd prime LTE and the 2-adic LTE variant apply in the stated hypotheses
  - Hensel lifting is applied only at simple roots (derivative not divisible by the prime)
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - Möbius inversion uniquely determines $f(n_0)$ and hence uniquely determines $t_2$, $t_p$, and $M$
  - The valuation profile of $M\pm 1$ together with 2-adic LTE and prime-power order lifting uniquely fixes the local orders and therefore fixes $r$ via CRT-lcm
  - Quadratic reciprocity determines the Legendre symbols needed for solvability, Hensel lifting fixes the prime-power root counts, and CRT fixes $s$; hence $r+s$ is unique
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: Dirichlet_convolution_optional
    inputs:
    - divisor-sum relations on divisors of $n_0$
    purpose: encode the hypothesis as a convolution equation
    outputs:
    - identity $F=f*\mathbf{1}$
  - name: mobius_inversion_optional
    inputs:
    - convolution equation $F=f*\mathbf{1}$
    purpose: invert the divisor-sum transform
    outputs:
    - formula $f=F*\mu$ and a Möbius sum for $f(n_0)$
  - name: mu_mobius_optional
    inputs:
    - Möbius sum over divisors of $n_0$
    purpose: reduce to squarefree divisors
    outputs:
    - 8-term inclusion-exclusion for $f(n_0)$ over $d\mid 2pq$
  - name: multiplicative_functions
    inputs:
    - uniform full-support evaluation of $F(n_0/d)$
    purpose: factor the Möbius sum into a product and a linear bracket
    outputs:
    - closed form for $f(n_0)$
  - name: v_p_valuation
    inputs:
    - $f(n_0)$
    purpose: extract $t_2=v_2(f(n_0))$ and $t_p=v_p(f(n_0))$ and form the unit $M$
    outputs:
    - integer $M$ coprime to $2p$ (and checked coprime to $q$)
  - name: two_adic_LTE
    inputs:
    - $v_2(M-1)$ and $v_2(M+1)$
    purpose: compute $\operatorname{ord}_{2^a}(M)$ exactly
    outputs:
    - $\operatorname{ord}_{2^a}(M)$
  - name: order_lifting_prime_powers
    inputs:
    - $\operatorname{ord}_\ell(M)$ and $v_\ell(M^{\operatorname{ord}_\ell(M)}-1)$ for odd primes $\ell$
    purpose: lift orders from $\ell$ to $\ell^e$
    outputs:
    - $\operatorname{ord}_{p^k}(M)$ and control over $\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(M)$
  - name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
    inputs:
    - coprime moduli $2^a$, $p^k$, $q^2$
    purpose: recombine local orders and root counts across components
    outputs:
    - $\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)=\operatorname{lcm}(\cdots)$ and root-count multiplicativity
  - name: quadratic_reciprocity
    inputs:
    - residue class of $M$ modulo $q$
    purpose: decide quadratic residuosity via Legendre symbols
    outputs:
    - solvability of $x^2\equiv M\pmod q$
  - name: Hensel_lifting
    inputs:
    - a simple square root modulo an odd prime
    purpose: lift square roots to prime powers and count them
    outputs:
    - exact square-root counts modulo $p^k$ and $q^2$
  theorem(s):
  - name: mobius_inversion
    role: gives $f=F*\mu$ from $F=f*\mathbf{1}$
  - name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE
    role: controls $v_\ell(a^{\ell^m}-1)$ for odd primes and supports order lifting
  - name: LTE_for_p_equals_2
    role: provides $v_2(a^{2^m}-1)=v_2(a-1)+v_2(a+1)+m-1$ for odd $a$
  - name: chinese_remainder_theorem
    role: supports the order-lcm relation and the multiplicativity of solution counts for coprime moduli
  - name: quadratic_reciprocity
    role: enables computation of Legendre symbols to decide quadratic residuosity
  - name: hensel_lemma
    role: guarantees unique lifting of simple roots to prime powers
  - name: euler_theorem
    role: guarantees existence of some $r>0$ with $M^r\equiv 1\pmod{n_0}$ when $\gcd(M,n_0)=1$
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - Divisor-sum transforms are inverted by Möbius inclusion-exclusion
  - Prime-power congruences are controlled by p-adic valuations and lifted in towers
  - Global constraints are reconstructed from local data by CRT
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - Squarefree collapse over three primes and uniform full-support evaluation gives a closed form for $f(n_0)$
  - Dual valuation extraction produces a unit $M$ that feeds both an order computation and a square-root count
  - 2-adic LTE and odd-prime order lifting determine local orders, recombined by CRT-lcm
  - Quadratic reciprocity and Hensel lifting determine prime-power root counts, recombined by CRT
  finish_type: closed_form
computational_context:
  preferred_language: python
  library(s):
  - name: math
    reason: gcd and modular exponentiation checks
  - name: sympy
    reason: Möbius inversion sums, multiplicative order, Legendre symbols, and modular square roots
  import_hint(s):
  - import math
  - import sympy as sp
  task(s):
  - name: independent_verification
    reason: Recompute $f(n_0)$ by Möbius inversion, extract $t_2,t_5,M$, compute $r=\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)$, and count square roots modulo $5^3\cdot 13^2$
well_posed: 'yes'

    q: 13

    C: 10

source_short_name: Mobius inversion with a two-sided valuation twist feeding an LTE plus CRT multiplicative-order computation
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: Dirichlet_convolution__mobius_inversion__double_twist__LTE_order__CRT_lcm
  instance_id: athena:number_theory_00:2026-02-25:inst_gen3_order_twist_3_11
  short_name: Mobius inversion with a two-sided valuation twist feeding an LTE plus CRT multiplicative-order computation
  generation: 3
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - arithmetic functions
  - Mobius inversion
  - p-adic valuation
  - lifting the exponent
  - multiplicative order
  - Chinese remainder theorem
  topic_path:
  - arithmetic_functions

  tags:

  - mobius_inversion_optional

  - lifting_the_exponent_LTE
  - multiplicative_order
  - Chinese_remainder_theorem
  - lcm_operator
  - gymnasium_ready
problem_spec:
  summary: On the divisor lattice of $n_0=p^k q^2$, define a valuation-sensitive function $F$ that adds one twist on multiples of $q$ and a second twist on multiples of $p$. Recover $f(n_0)$ uniquely via Mobius inversion, extract the $p$-adic factor $t=v_p(f(n_0))$ and $M=f(n_0)/p^t$, then compute the multiplicative order of $M$ modulo $n_0$ by combining LTE-based order lifting on $p^k$ and $q^2$ with a CRT-lcm recombination.
  object(s):
  - name: p
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: k
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: A
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: B
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: n_0
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: dependent
  - name: F
    type: function
    role: given
    dependency: dependent
  - name: f
    type: function
    role: unknown
    dependency: dependent
  - name: t
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: M
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: r
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes and $k\ge 3$
  - $n_0=p^k q^2$
  - $A$ and $B$ are integers with $\mathrm{gcd}(A,q)=1$ and $\mathrm{gcd}(B,p)=1$
  - 'For each divisor $n\mid n_0$ with $i=v_p(n)$ and $j=v_q(n)$, define $F$ by the rules: $F(1)=1$; if $j=0$ and $i\ge 1$ then $F(p^i)=p^i+Bp^{i-1}$; if $i=0$ and $j\ge 1$ then $F(q^j)=q^j+Aq^{j-1}$; if $i\ge 1$ and $j\ge 1$ then $F(p^i q^j)=p^i q^j + A p^i q^{j-1} + B p^{i-1} q^j$'
  - There exists an arithmetic function $f$ on divisors of $n_0$ such that for every $n\mid n_0$, $F(n)=\sum_{d\mid n} f(d)$
  - Define $t=v_p(f(n_0))$ and $M=f(n_0)/p^t$
  ask:
  - Compute $f(n_0)$ and determine $t$ and $M$
  - Determine the least positive integer $r$ such that $M^r\equiv 1(\mathrm{mod}\ n_0)$
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: none
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p
    type: int
    domain: odd prime
    purpose: base prime for the $p^k$ component and $p$-adic order lifting
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime with $q\ne p$
    purpose: base prime for the $q^2$ component and $q$-adic order lifting
  - name: k
    type: int
    domain: integer with $k\ge 3$
    purpose: depth of the prime-power modulus $p^k$
  - name: A
    type: int
    domain: integer with $\mathrm{gcd}(A,q)=1$
    purpose: $q$-side valuation twist in $F$
  - name: B
    type: int
    domain: integer with $\mathrm{gcd}(B,p)=1$
    purpose: $p$-side valuation twist in $F$
  invariant(s):
  - name: convolution_encoding
    depends_on:
    - n_0
    statement: $F(n)=\sum_{d\mid n} f(d)$ is equivalent to $F=f*\mathbf{1}$ under Dirichlet convolution on divisors of $n_0$
  - name: mobius_inverse_of_1
    depends_on:
    - n_0
    statement: $\mu*\mathbf{1}=\varepsilon$ on the divisor lattice, so $f$ is uniquely determined by $f=F*\mu$
  - name: squarefree_reduction_for_p_k_q2
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    - k
    statement: For $n_0=p^k q^2$, only squarefree divisors contribute in Mobius inversion, so $f(n_0)=F(n_0)-F(n_0/p)-F(n_0/q)+F(n_0/(pq))$
  - name: LTE_valuation_growth
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    statement: For an odd prime $\ell$ with $\ell\mid (a-1)$, LTE gives $v_\ell(a^{\ell^m}-1)=v_\ell(a-1)+m$
  - name: order_lcm_under_CRT
    depends_on:
    - p
    - q
    - k
    statement: If $\mathrm{gcd}(m,n)=1$ and $\mathrm{gcd}(a,mn)=1$ then $\mathrm{ord}_{mn}(a)=\mathrm{lcm}(\mathrm{ord}_m(a),\mathrm{ord}_n(a))$
  operator(s):
  - Dirichlet_convolution_optional
  - mobius_inversion_optional
  - mu_mobius_optional
  - v_p_valuation
  - lifting_the_exponent_LTE
  - multiplicative_order
  - Chinese_remainder_theorem
  - lcm_operator
  essential_assumption(s):
  - Mobius inversion applies on the divisor lattice, so $F=f*\mathbf{1}$ implies $f=F*\mu$
  - The computed $M$ is coprime to $n_0$, so its multiplicative order modulo $n_0$ is defined
  - $p$ and $q$ are odd primes so LTE applies in the form used
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - Mobius inversion uniquely determines $f(n_0)$ and therefore uniquely determines $t$ and $M$
  - Lemma 2 fixes the local orders from $v_p(M-1)$ and $v_q(M-1)$, and Lemma 3 then fixes the global order $r$ uniquely
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: Dirichlet_convolution_optional
    inputs:
    - divisor-sum relations on divisors of $n_0$
    purpose: encode the hypothesis as a convolution equation
    outputs:
    - identity $F=f*\mathbf{1}$
  - name: mobius_inversion_optional
    inputs:
    - convolution equation $F=f*\mathbf{1}$
    purpose: invert the divisor-sum transform
    outputs:
    - formula $f=F*\mu$ and a sum for $f(n_0)$
  - name: mu_mobius_optional
    inputs:
    - Mobius sum over divisors
    purpose: reduce to squarefree divisors
    outputs:
    - four-term inclusion-exclusion for $f(n_0)$
  - name: multiplicative_functions
    inputs:
    - valuation-dependent definition of $F$ on $p^i q^j$
    purpose: evaluate $F$ on the required divisors and simplify
    outputs:
    - explicit integer $f(n_0)$
  - name: v_p_valuation
    inputs:
    - $f(n_0)$
    purpose: extract $t=v_p(f(n_0))$ and $M=f(n_0)/p^t$
    outputs:
    - the unit $M$ modulo $n_0$
  - name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE
    inputs:
    - $v_p(M-1)$ and $v_q(M-1)$
    purpose: compute local orders modulo prime powers
    outputs:
    - $\mathrm{ord}_{p^k}(M)$ and $\mathrm{ord}_{q^2}(M)$
  - name: multiplicative_order
    inputs:
    - $M$ modulo $n_0$
    purpose: compute the least $r$ with $M^r\equiv 1(\mathrm{mod}\ n_0)$
    outputs:
    - $r$
  - name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
    inputs:
    - coprime moduli $p^k$ and $q^2$
    purpose: recombine local orders into the global order using $\mathrm{lcm}$
    outputs:
    - $\mathrm{ord}_{n_0}(M)=\mathrm{lcm}(\mathrm{ord}_{p^k}(M),\mathrm{ord}_{q^2}(M))$
  theorem(s):
  - name: mobius_inversion
    role: gives $f=F*\mu$ from $F=f*\mathbf{1}$
  - name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE
    role: provides $v_\ell(a^{\ell^m}-1)=v_\ell(a-1)+m$ for odd $\ell\mid (a-1)$
  - name: chinese_remainder_theorem
    role: supports the order-lcm relation for coprime moduli
  - name: euler_theorem
    role: guarantees existence of some $r>0$ with $M^r\equiv 1(\mathrm{mod}\ n_0)$ when $\mathrm{gcd}(M,n_0)=1$
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - Divisor-sum transforms are inverted by Mobius inclusion-exclusion
  - Orders modulo coprime moduli are controlled by prime-power behavior and recombined by $\mathrm{lcm}$
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - Use squarefree collapse to compute $f(n_0)$ from four values of $F$
  - Use LTE to obtain exact local orders from $v_p(M-1)$ and $v_q(M-1)$, then use CRT-lcm to obtain the global order
  finish_type: closed_form
computational_context:
  preferred_language: python
  library(s):
  - name: math
    reason: gcd and pow for modular checks
  - name: sympy
    reason: mobius and multiplicative order computations for independent verification
  import_hint(s):
  - import math
  - import sympy as sp
  task(s):
  - name: independent_verification
    reason: Compute $f(n_0)$ by Mobius inversion, extract $t$ and $M$, then compute the order of $M$ modulo $n_0$ and confirm minimality
well_posed: 'yes'

    p: 3
    q: 11
    k: 4

    B: 1
  final_answer:
    value: 297
    normalized: 297

source_short_name: Order modulo $2^m p_1 p_2$ forced by reciprocity, LTE, and CRT
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: quadratic_residues__safe_prime_pair__2power_extension__CRT_order_lcm__LTE_2adic
  instance_id: number_theory_nt_14_gen3_instance_2026_02_25_a
  short_name: Order modulo $2^m p_1 p_2$ forced by reciprocity, LTE, and CRT
  generation: 3
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - quadratic residues
  - modular arithmetic
  - multiplicative order
  - Chinese remainder theorem
  - p-adic valuation
  - lifting the exponent
  topic_path:
  - quadratic_residues
  - Legendre_symbol
  - Euler_criterion
  - CRT_order_composition
  - two_adic_order_lifting
  tags:
  - Legendre_symbol
  - quadratic_reciprocity
  - supplementary_laws
  - Euler_criterion
  - multiplicative_order
  - safe_primes
  - Chinese_remainder_theorem
  - lcm_of_orders
  - LTE
  - two_adic_valuation
  - order_mod_2_power
problem_spec:
  summary: Given safe primes $p_1=2r_1+1$ and $p_2=2r_2+1$ and an odd prime $q$ with $q\mid(p_1-2)$ and $q\mid(p_2+2)$, quadratic reciprocity and supplementary laws determine $(q/p_1)$ and $(q/p_2)$. Euler's criterion then forces $\operatorname{ord}_{p_1}(q)$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{p_2}(q)$. A $2^m$ factor is adjoined to the modulus, and LTE at $p=2$ forces $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q)$. CRT implies the global order modulo $2^m p_1 p_2$ is the least common multiple of these local orders, and the output is $k$ reduced modulo $1000$.
  object(s):
  - name: p1
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: p2
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: m
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: r1
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: r2
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: n
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k0
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k1
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k2
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  - name: N
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - Let $p_1,p_2$ be distinct odd primes and let $r_1=(p_1-1)/2$ and $r_2=(p_2-1)/2$ be primes.
  - Let $q$ be an odd prime with $q\mid(p_1-2)$ and $q\mid(p_2+2)$.
  - Let $m\ge 3$ and set $n=2^m p_1 p_2$.
  - Define $k=\operatorname{ord}_n(q)$ as the least positive integer with $q^k\equiv 1\pmod n$.
  ask:
  - Determine the unique integer $N$ with $0\le N<1000$ and $N\equiv k\pmod{1000}$.
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p1
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p_1$ with $(p_1-1)/2$ also prime
    purpose: safe prime modulus giving $p_1-1=2r_1$ with $r_1$ prime
  - name: p2
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p_2\ne p_1$ with $(p_2-1)/2$ also prime
    purpose: second safe prime modulus giving $p_2-1=2r_2$ with $r_2$ prime
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $q$ satisfying $q\mid(p_1-2)$ and $q\mid(p_2+2)$ and $q\equiv 3\pmod 8$
    purpose: forces opposite quadratic character at $p_1$ and $p_2$ and forces a maximal two-adic order via LTE
  - name: m
    type: int
    domain: integer $m\ge 3$
    purpose: introduces a $2^m$ factor so the order problem requires a two-adic lifting phase
  invariant(s):
  - name: safe_prime_local_order_sieve
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    statement: For $i\in\{1,2\}$, Fermat's little theorem gives $q^{p_i-1}\equiv 1\pmod{p_i}$, so $\operatorname{ord}_{p_i}(q)\mid(p_i-1)=2r_i$, hence $\operatorname{ord}_{p_i}(q)\in\{1,2,r_i,2r_i\}$. Since $1<q<p_i$, the cases $1$ and $2$ are excluded, so $\operatorname{ord}_{p_i}(q)\in\{r_i,2r_i\}$.
  - name: reciprocity_split_via_pm2
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    statement: From $p_1\equiv 2\pmod q$ and $p_2\equiv -2\pmod q$ and the oddness of $(p_i-1)/2$, quadratic reciprocity yields $(q/p_1)=(-2/q)$ and $(q/p_2)=(2/q)$.
  - name: Euler_character_to_local_order
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    statement: Euler's criterion gives $q^{(p_i-1)/2}\equiv(q/p_i)\pmod{p_i}$, so $(q/p_i)=1 forces $\operatorname{ord}_{p_i}(q)=r_i$ and $(q/p_i)=-1$ forces $\operatorname{ord}_{p_i}(q)=2r_i$ under the safe prime local sieve.
  - name: two_power_order_via_LTE
    depends_on:
    - q
    - m
    statement: If $q\equiv 3\pmod 8$ and $t\ge 1$, then LTE at $p=2$ gives $v_2(q^{2^t}-1)=v_2(q-1)+v_2(q+1)+t-1=t+2$, so $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q)=2^{m-2}$.
  - name: CRT_lcm_global_order_three_factors
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    - m
    statement: Since $2^m$, $p_1$, and $p_2$ are pairwise coprime, CRT implies $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m p_1 p_2}(q)=\operatorname{lcm}(\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q),\operatorname{ord}_{p_1}(q),\operatorname{ord}_{p_2}(q))$.
  operator(s):
  - Legendre_symbol
  - quadratic_reciprocity
  - supplementary_laws
  - Euler_criterion
  - multiplicative_order
  - Chinese_remainder_theorem
  - lcm_operation
  - two_adic_valuation
  - lifting_the_exponent
  - mod_m_normalization
  essential_assumption(s):
  - All of $p_1,p_2,q$ are primes and $\gcd(q,2p_1p_2)=1$ so all local and global multiplicative orders exist.
  - The coupled congruences $q\mid(p_1-2)$ and $q\mid(p_2+2)$ are required to reduce $(q/p_1)$ and $(q/p_2)$ to $(\pm 2/q)$ via quadratic reciprocity.
  - The condition $q\equiv 3\pmod 8$ is required so that LTE forces the exact two-adic valuation growth and hence forces $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q)$.
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - 'Local uniqueness at $p_1$ and $p_2$: the safe prime sieve restricts the order to $\{r_i,2r_i\}$, and quadratic reciprocity plus supplementary laws fix $(q/p_i)$, so Euler''s criterion forces a unique branch.'
  - 'Two-adic uniqueness: LTE yields an exact valuation formula for $v_2(q^{2^t}-1)$, forcing the smallest exponent giving $q^{2^t}\equiv 1\pmod{2^m}$ and hence forcing $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q)$.'
  - 'Global uniqueness: CRT implies the global order is the least common multiple of the forced local orders, and the normalization rule $0\le N<1000$, $N\equiv k\pmod{1000}$ then forces a unique output.'
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: safe_prime_order_dichotomy
    inputs:
    - a safe prime $p=2r+1$ and a unit $q$ modulo $p$
    purpose: reduce $\operatorname{ord}_p(q)$ to a small candidate set using $p-1=2r$
    outputs:
    - the candidate set $\operatorname{ord}_p(q)\in\{r,2r\}$ after excluding $1$ and $2$
  - name: quadratic_reciprocity
    inputs:
    - odd primes $p,q$
    purpose: compute $(q/p_i)$ from $(p_i/q)$ when $(p_i-1)/2$ is odd
    outputs:
    - reductions $(q/p_1)=(-2/q)$ and $(q/p_2)=(2/q)$
  - name: supplementary_laws
    inputs:
    - odd prime $q$
    purpose: evaluate $(2/q)$ and $(-1/q)$ from $q\bmod 8$
    outputs:
    - explicit values of $(2/q)$ and $(-2/q)$
  - name: Euler_criterion
    inputs:
    - odd prime modulus $p$ and unit $q$
    purpose: convert quadratic character into a forced congruence for $q^{(p-1)/2}$
    outputs:
    - forced choice of $\operatorname{ord}_{p}(q)$ under the safe prime sieve
  - name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE_p_equals_2
    inputs:
    - odd integer $q$ and exponent $2^t$
    purpose: compute $v_2(q^{2^t}-1)$ exactly
    outputs:
    - two-adic valuation growth that forces $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q)$
  - name: CRT_order_lcm
    inputs:
    - pairwise coprime moduli and the corresponding local orders
    purpose: deduce $\operatorname{ord}_{m_1m_2m_3}(q)=\operatorname{lcm}(\operatorname{ord}_{m_1}(q),\operatorname{ord}_{m_2}(q),\operatorname{ord}_{m_3}(q))$
    outputs:
    - a closed form for the global order
  - name: mod_m_normalization
    inputs:
    - integer $k$ and modulus $1000$
    purpose: produce the unique representative $N$ with $0\le N<1000$ and $N\equiv k\pmod{1000}$
    outputs:
    - final answer $N$
  theorem(s):
  - name: Fermat_little_theorem
    role: gives $q^{p-1}\equiv 1\pmod p$ for primes $p\nmid q$ and implies $\operatorname{ord}_p(q)\mid(p-1)$.
  - name: quadratic_reciprocity
    role: relates $(q/p)$ and $(p/q)$ with the correct sign.
  - name: Euler_criterion
    role: gives $q^{(p-1)/2}\equiv(q/p)\pmod p$.
  - name: LTE_p_equals_2
    role: gives $v_2(a^n-b^n)=v_2(a-b)+v_2(a+b)+v_2(n)-1$ for odd $a,b$ and even $n$.
  - name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
    role: gives equivalence of congruences modulo $2^m p_1 p_2$ with congruences modulo $2^m$, $p_1$, and $p_2$.
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - Quadratic residue information determines whether $q^{(p-1)/2}$ is $1$ or $-1$ modulo $p$, which forces the order when $p-1=2r$ with $r$ prime.
  - Two-adic valuation growth controls when $q^{2^t}\equiv 1\pmod{2^m}$.
  - CRT turns a global order condition into simultaneous local order conditions.

  critical_trick(s):
  - Use the coupled congruences $p_1\equiv 2\pmod q$ and $p_2\equiv -2\pmod q$ to force opposite values of $(q/p_1)$ and $(q/p_2)$.
  - Use LTE at $p=2$ to compute $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q)$.

source_short_name: Order modulo $2^m5^a p_1p_2$ with $(m,a)$ forced by central binomial valuations
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: quadratic_residues__safe_prime_pair__binomial_valuations_define_2_5_exponents__CRT_order_lcm__LTE_2adic__LTE_oddprime_5adic
  instance_id: number_theory_gen4_2026_02_25_nt_14_4
  short_name: Order modulo $2^m5^a p_1p_2$ with $(m,a)$ forced by central binomial valuations
  generation: 4
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - quadratic residues
  - modular arithmetic
  - multiplicative order
  - Chinese remainder theorem
  - p-adic valuation

  - binomial/factorial divisibility
  - prime-power order lifting

problem_spec:
  summary: A safe-prime pair $(p_1,p_2)$ and a prime $q$ with $p_1\equiv 2\pmod q$, $p_2\equiv -2\pmod q$ force opposite quadratic character of $q$ at $p_1$ and $p_2$ via reciprocity; Euler's criterion then fixes the local orders. The modulus also includes $2^m$ and $5^a$, where $m=v_2\binom{2r_1}{r_1}$ and $a=v_5\binom{2r_2}{r_2}$ are computed from base-$2$ and base-$5$ digit/carry invariants (Kummer/Legendre). LTE at $2$ and at $5$ forces the prime-power local orders, and CRT yields the global order as an lcm. The output is $(k+m+a)\bmod 1000.
  object(s):
  - name: p1
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: p2
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: r1
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: r2
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: m
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: a
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: n
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: N
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - Let $p_1,p_2$ be distinct odd primes and let $r_1=(p_1-1)/2$ and $r_2=(p_2-1)/2$ be primes.
  - Let $q$ be an odd prime with $q\mid(p_1-2)$ and $q\mid(p_2+2)$.
  - Define $m=v_2\binom{2r_1}{r_1}$ and $a=v_5\binom{2r_2}{r_2}$, and set $n=2^m5^a p_1p_2$.
  - Define $k=\operatorname{ord}_n(q)$ as the least positive integer with $q^k\equiv 1\pmod n$.
  ask:
  - Determine the unique integer $N$ with $0\le N<1000$ and $N\equiv k+m+a\pmod{1000}$.
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p1
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p_1=2r_1+1$ with $r_1$ prime and $p_1\equiv 2\pmod q$
    purpose: safe-prime factor forcing $\operatorname{ord}_{p_1}(q)\in\{r_1,2r_1\}$ and linking $m$ to $r_1$ via $v_2\binom{2r_1}{r_1}$
  - name: p2
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $p_2=2r_2+1$ with $r_2$ prime and $p_2\equiv -2\pmod q$
    purpose: second safe-prime factor forcing $\operatorname{ord}_{p_2}(q)\in\{r_2,2r_2\}$ and linking $a$ to $r_2$ via $v_5\binom{2r_2}{r_2}$
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime $q$ with $q\equiv 3\pmod 8$, $\gcd(q,10p_1p_2)=1$, $\operatorname{ord}_5(q)=4$, and $v_5(q^4-1)=1$
    purpose: forces opposite quadratic character at $p_1,p_2$, yields controlled two-adic order via LTE at $2$, and yields controlled order lifting on $5^a$ via LTE at $5$
  invariant(s):
  - name: safe_prime_local_order_sieve
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    statement: For $i\in\{1,2\}$, $\operatorname{ord}_{p_i}(q)\mid(p_i-1)=2r_i$ and $q\not\equiv\pm 1\pmod{p_i}$, hence $\operatorname{ord}_{p_i}(q)\in\{r_i,2r_i\}$.
  - name: reciprocity_split_via_pm2
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    statement: From $p_1\equiv 2\pmod q$, $p_2\equiv -2\pmod q$, and oddness of $r_i=(p_i-1)/2$, quadratic reciprocity implies $(q/p_1)=(-2/q)$ and $(q/p_2)=(2/q)$.
  - name: Euler_character_to_local_order
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    statement: Euler's criterion gives $q^{(p_i-1)/2}\equiv(q/p_i)\pmod{p_i}$, so under the safe-prime sieve $(q/p_i)=1$ forces $\operatorname{ord}_{p_i}(q)=r_i$ and $(q/p_i)=-1$ forces $\operatorname{ord}_{p_i}(q)=2r_i$.
  - name: m_from_central_binomial_v2
    depends_on:
    - r1
    statement: Kummer's theorem at $p=2$ yields $m=v_2\binom{2r_1}{r_1}$ equals the number of carries in $r_1+r_1$ in base $2$, equivalently the number of $1$ bits of $r_1$.
  - name: a_from_central_binomial_v5
    depends_on:
    - r2
    statement: Legendre's digit-sum formula implies $a=v_5\binom{2r_2}{r_2}=\frac{2s_5(r_2)-s_5(2r_2)}{4}$; equivalently, by Kummer, $a$ equals the number of carries in $r_2+r_2$ in base $5$.
  - name: two_power_order_via_LTE
    depends_on:
    - q
    - m
    statement: If $q\equiv 3\pmod 8$, then $v_2(q^{2^t}-1)=t+2$, so $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q)=2^{m-2}$ for $m\ge 3$.
  - name: five_power_order_lift_via_LTE
    depends_on:
    - q
    - a
    statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_5(q)=4$ and $v_5(q^4-1)=1$, then LTE at $p=5$ gives $\operatorname{ord}_{5^a}(q)=4\cdot 5^{a-1}$.
  - name: CRT_lcm_global_order_four_factors
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    - m
    - a
    statement: Since $2^m,5^a,p_1,p_2$ are pairwise coprime, CRT implies $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m5^ap_1p_2}(q)=\operatorname{lcm}(\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q),\operatorname{ord}_{5^a}(q),\operatorname{ord}_{p_1}(q),\operatorname{ord}_{p_2}(q))$.
  operator(s):
  - Legendre_symbol
  - quadratic_reciprocity
  - supplementary_laws
  - Euler_criterion
  - multiplicative_order
  - Chinese_remainder_theorem
  - lcm_operation
  - two_adic_valuation
  - five_adic_valuation
  - lifting_the_exponent
  - Kummer_carries_operator
  - Legendre_factorial_valuation
  - base_p_digit_sum
  - mod_m_normalization
  essential_assumption(s):
  - All of $p_1,p_2,q$ are primes and $\gcd(q,10p_1p_2)=1$ so the relevant multiplicative orders exist.
  - The coupled congruences $q\mid(p_1-2)$ and $q\mid(p_2+2)$ are required to force $(q/p_1)$ and $(q/p_2)$ via quadratic reciprocity.
  - The lifting guard $\operatorname{ord}_5(q)=4$ and $v_5(q^4-1)=1$ is required so that LTE at $5$ forces $\operatorname{ord}_{5^a}(q)=4\cdot 5^{a-1}$.
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - 'Binomial-valuation uniqueness: $m$ and $a$ are uniquely forced from $(r_1,r_2)$ using Kummer/Legendre digit invariants; thus $2^m$ and $5^a$ are uniquely determined.'
  - 'Local order uniqueness at $p_1,p_2$: the safe-prime sieve restricts the order to $\{r_i,2r_i\}$ and reciprocity plus supplementary laws fix $(q/p_i)$, so Euler''s criterion forces a unique branch for each local order.'
  - 'Prime-power uniqueness: LTE at $2$ fixes $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q)$, and LTE at $5$ plus the lifting guard fixes $\operatorname{ord}_{5^a}(q)$.'
  - 'Global uniqueness: CRT forces the global order to be the least common multiple of the uniquely forced local orders, and the normalization $0\le N<1000$ with $N\equiv k+m+a\pmod{1000}$ then forces a unique output.'
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: Kummer_theorem_carries
    inputs:
    - central binomial coefficient $\binom{2r_1}{r_1}$
    purpose: compute $m=v_2\binom{2r_1}{r_1}$ via carries/bit-counting
    outputs:
    - $m$ from the binary expansion of $r_1$
  - name: Legendre_formula_digit_sum
    inputs:
    - factorials $(2r_2)!$ and $r_2!$
    purpose: compute $a=v_5\binom{2r_2}{r_2}$ via base-$5$ digit sums
    outputs:
    - $a$ from $s_5(r_2)$ and $s_5(2r_2)$
  - name: safe_prime_order_dichotomy
    inputs:
    - safe primes $p_1,p_2$ and unit $q$
    purpose: restrict each $\operatorname{ord}_{p_i}(q)$ to $\{r_i,2r_i\}$
    outputs:
    - two-point candidate sets for local orders
  - name: quadratic_reciprocity
    inputs:
    - odd primes $p_i,q$ with $(p_i-1)/2$ odd
    purpose: reduce $(q/p_i)$ to $(\pm2/q)$
    outputs:
    - explicit Legendre symbols $(q/p_1)$ and $(q/p_2)$
  - name: supplementary_laws
    inputs:
    - prime $q$
    purpose: evaluate $(2/q)$ and $(-1/q)$ from $q\bmod 8$
    outputs:
    - numerical values of $(2/q)$ and $(-2/q)$
  - name: Euler_criterion
    inputs:
    - odd prime $p$ and unit $q$
    purpose: convert Legendre symbols into forced congruences and orders
    outputs:
    - forced local orders at $p_1,p_2$
  - name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE_p_equals_2
    inputs:
    - odd integer $q$ and exponents $2^t$
    purpose: compute $v_2(q^{2^t}-1)$ exactly
    outputs:
    - two-adic order $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(q)$
  - name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE_odd_prime
    inputs:
    - odd prime $5$ and powers $5^a$
    purpose: lift $5$-adic valuations and deduce order growth modulo $5^a$
    outputs:
    - order $\operatorname{ord}_{5^a}(q)$
  - name: CRT_order_lcm
    inputs:
    - pairwise coprime moduli $2^m,5^a,p_1,p_2$
    purpose: combine local orders into the global order by lcm
    outputs:
    - global order $k=\operatorname{ord}_n(q)$
  - name: mod_m_normalization_with_offset
    inputs:
    - integer $k+m+a$ and modulus $1000$
    purpose: compute the unique representative $N\in[0,1000)$
    outputs:
    - final answer $N$
  theorem(s):
  - name: Fermat_little_theorem
    role: implies $\operatorname{ord}_p(q)\mid(p-1)$ for prime $p\nmid q$.
  - name: Kummer_theorem
    role: identifies $v_p\binom{N}{K}$ with carry counts in base $p$.
  - name: Legendre_formula
    role: computes $v_p(N!)$ and enables binomial valuations via digit sums.
  - name: quadratic_reciprocity
    role: relates $(q/p)$ and $(p/q)$ with a controlled sign.
  - name: Euler_criterion
    role: gives $q^{(p-1)/2}\equiv(q/p)\pmod p$.
  - name: LTE_p_equals_2
    role: gives exact $2$-adic valuation growth for $q^{2^t}-1$.
  - name: LTE_odd_prime
    role: gives $5$-adic valuation lifting for $q^{d\cdot 5^t}-1$.
  - name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
    role: equates congruences modulo the product with congruences modulo each coprime factor.
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - Quadratic character controls $q^{(p-1)/2}\bmod p$, forcing the order when $p-1=2r$ with $r$ prime.
  - Carry/digit invariants encode $p$-adic valuations of binomial coefficients.
  - Exact $p$-adic valuation growth (LTE) determines multiplicative orders modulo prime powers.
  - CRT composes local conditions into a global order via an lcm.
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - Define $m$ and $a$ by binomial valuations so digit/carry invariants are necessary before order computations.
  - Use $p_1\equiv 2\pmod q$ and $p_2\equiv -2\pmod q$ to force opposite quadratic character at the two safe primes.
  - Use LTE at $2$ and at $5$ to pin down prime-power orders, then combine by CRT.
  finish_type: closed_form
computational_context:
  preferred_language: python
  library(s):
  - name: sympy
    reason: independent verification of primality and multiplicative orders (n_order).
  - name: math
    reason: gcd/lcm computations and modular reductions.
  import_hint(s):
  - import math

  task(s):

    reason: check $p_1,p_2,q,r_1,r_2$ are prime and that $q\mid(p_1-2)$ and $q\mid(p_2+2)$.
  - name: verification_m_a_via_valuation_formulas
    reason: compute $m$ via popcount and $a$ via factorial valuation (Legendre) without constructing huge binomials.
  - name: verification_orders_and_final_normalization
    reason: confirm $\operatorname{ord}_n(q)$ via n_order and compute $(k+m+a)\bmod 1000$.

source_short_name: Order modulo product of safe primes via reciprocity split and CRT-lcm
text:
Athena Canon v2.1 breakdown record. Read this as problem-decomposition memory: identify the objects, givens, ask, invariants, techniques, critical trick, finish type, and answer-normalization contract.

schema_id: distillator_dsl.math.v2.1
schema_version: 1
identity:
  family_id: quadratic_residues__safe_prime_pair__CRT_order_lcm__reciprocity_split
  instance_id: number_theory_nt00_gen2_realization_2026-02-24_nonce_c1f5a7
  short_name: Order modulo product of safe primes via reciprocity split and CRT-lcm
  generation: 2
taxonomy:
  domain: number_theory
  subdomain(s):
  - quadratic residues
  - modular arithmetic
  - multiplicative order
  - Chinese remainder theorem
  topic_path:
  - quadratic_residues
  - Legendre_symbol
  - CRT_order_composition
  tags:
  - Euler_criterion
  - Legendre_symbol
  - quadratic_reciprocity
  - supplementary_laws
  - multiplicative_order
  - safe_primes
  - Chinese_remainder_theorem
  - lcm_of_orders
  - gymnasium_ready
  - material_novelty
problem_spec:
  summary: Given two safe primes p1=2r1+1 and p2=2r2+1 and an odd prime q simultaneously constrained by q|(p1-2) and q|(p2+2), quadratic reciprocity reduces (q/p1) and (q/p2) to the supplementary evaluations of (-2/q) and (2/q), which via Euler's criterion force the local orders ord_{p1}(q) and ord_{p2}(q). The global order modulo n=p1 p2 is then forced by a CRT-lcm composition argument, and the output is the reduction modulo 1000.
  object(s):
  - name: p1
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: p2
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: q
    type: integer
    role: given
    dependency: independent
  - name: r1
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: r2
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: n
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k1
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k2
    type: integer
    role: aux
    dependency: dependent
  - name: k
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  - name: N
    type: integer
    role: output
    dependency: dependent
  givens:
  - Let p1,p2 be odd primes such that r1=(p1-1)/2 and r2=(p2-1)/2 are also odd primes (so p1,p2 are safe primes).
  - Let q be an odd prime such that q|(p1-2) and q|(p2+2).
  - Let n=p1 p2 and define k=ord_n(q), the least positive integer with q^k ≡ 1 (mod n).
  ask:
  - (i) Using quadratic reciprocity together with p1 ≡ 2 (mod q) and p2 ≡ -2 (mod q), reduce (q/p1) and (q/p2) to explicit evaluations of (-2/q) and (2/q) (hence to conditions on q mod 8).
  - (ii) Use Euler's criterion and the safe-prime structure to determine k1=ord_{p1}(q) and k2=ord_{p2}(q).
  - (iii) Use the Chinese remainder theorem to prove k=ord_{p1 p2}(q)=lcm(k1,k2) and report the normalized answer N defined by 0 ≤ N < 1000 and N ≡ k (mod 1000).
answer_spec:
  type: integer
  normalization: mod_m
  modulus: 1000
family_structure:
  parameter(s):
  - name: p1
    type: int
    domain: odd prime p1 such that r1=(p1-1)/2 is an odd prime (safe prime)
    purpose: first prime modulus; its safe-prime factorization forces a sharp local order dichotomy
  - name: p2
    type: int
    domain: odd prime p2 such that r2=(p2-1)/2 is an odd prime (safe prime), with p2 != p1
    purpose: second prime modulus; provides a second local order dichotomy whose combination is nontrivial
  - name: q
    type: int
    domain: odd prime q with q|(p1-2) and q|(p2+2)
    purpose: 'forces two distinct reciprocity collapses: (q/p1)→(-2/q) and (q/p2)→(2/q)'
  invariant(s):
  - name: safe_prime_local_order_sieve
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    statement: With p_i=2r_i+1 and r_i prime, (Z/p_i Z)^× is cyclic of order 2r_i, so any element order lies in {1,2,r_i,2r_i}; excluding q ≡ ±1 (mod p_i) yields ord_{p_i}(q) ∈ {r_i,2r_i}.
  - name: reciprocity_split_via_pm2
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    statement: Because p1 ≡ 2 (mod q) and p2 ≡ -2 (mod q) with p1 ≡ p2 ≡ 3 (mod 4), quadratic reciprocity gives (q/p1)=(-1/q)(2/q)=(-2/q) and (q/p2)=(-1/q)(-2/q)=(2/q), reducing both Legendre symbols to supplementary evaluations from q mod 8.
  - name: Euler_character_to_local_order
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    statement: Euler's criterion yields q^{r_i} ≡ (q/p_i) (mod p_i), so (q/p_i)=1 forces ord_{p_i}(q)=r_i while (q/p_i)=-1 forces ord_{p_i}(q)=2r_i under the safe-prime local sieve.
  - name: CRT_lcm_global_order
    depends_on:
    - p1
    - p2
    - q
    statement: For n=p1 p2 with gcd(p1,p2)=1, the Chinese remainder theorem implies q^t ≡ 1 (mod n) iff q^t ≡ 1 (mod p1) and (mod p2); hence ord_n(q)=lcm(ord_{p1}(q),ord_{p2}(q)).
  operator(s):
  - Legendre_symbol
  - quadratic_reciprocity
  - supplementary_laws
  - Euler_criterion
  - multiplicative_order
  - Chinese_remainder_theorem
  - lcm_operation
  - mod_m_normalization
  essential_assumption(s):
  - 'Prime-mode: p1,p2 are distinct safe primes so that each local unit group has order 2r_i with r_i prime, enabling a two-branch local order classification.'
  - 'Link is structural: the coupled congruences q|(p1-2) and q|(p2+2) are used to force a reciprocity split into (-2/q) and (2/q); removing either link breaks deterministic computation of both local quadratic characters.'
  - 'Nontriviality guard: since 1<q<p_i, we automatically avoid q ≡ ±1 (mod p_i), preventing local orders 1 or 2 and forcing the argument to pass through quadratic-character evaluation and CRT composition.'
  uniqueness_certificate(s):
  - 'Local uniqueness: safe-prime structure plus the computed Legendre symbol (q/p_i) ∈ {±1} forces a unique local order ord_{p_i}(q) ∈ {r_i,2r_i} via Euler''s criterion.'
  - 'Global uniqueness: CRT implies ord_{p1 p2}(q)=lcm(ord_{p1}(q),ord_{p2}(q)), uniquely determining k and hence uniquely determining N ≡ k (mod 1000) with 0 ≤ N < 1000.'
reasoning_components:
  technique(s):
  - name: safe_prime_order_dichotomy
    inputs:
    - safe primes p_i=2r_i+1
    purpose: reduce each local multiplicative order to the two candidates r_i or 2r_i after excluding trivial residues
    outputs:
    - local candidate set ord_{p_i}(q) ∈ {r_i,2r_i}
  - name: multiplicative_order
    inputs:
    - unit group modulo a prime or coprime modulus
    purpose: formalize k=ord_m(a) and derive divisibility constraints from group order
    outputs:
    - divisor restrictions and minimality properties for orders
  - name: quadratic_reciprocity
    inputs:
    - odd primes p_i,q with p_i ≡ 3 (mod 4)
    purpose: swap (q/p_i) to (p_i/q) with the correct sign, then use the forced congruences p1 ≡ 2 (mod q) and p2 ≡ -2 (mod q)
    outputs:
    - reductions (q/p1)=(-2/q) and (q/p2)=(2/q)
  - name: supplementary_laws
    inputs:
    - odd prime q
    purpose: evaluate (2/q) and (-2/q) via q mod 8 to finish the reciprocity computation
    outputs:
    - explicit (q/p1) and (q/p2) values
  - name: Euler_criterion
    inputs:
    - odd prime modulus p and unit q
    purpose: 'bridge quadratic character to exponent: q^{(p-1)/2} ≡ (q/p) (mod p)'
    outputs:
    - forced local order choice under the safe-prime dichotomy
  - name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
    inputs:
    - coprime moduli p1,p2
    purpose: translate congruences modulo p1 p2 into simultaneous congruences modulo p1 and p2
    outputs:
    - CRT equivalence used to compose local order information
  - name: CRT_order_lcm
    inputs:
    - local orders k1=ord_{p1}(q) and k2=ord_{p2}(q)
    purpose: use CRT plus order minimality to prove ord_{p1 p2}(q)=lcm(k1,k2) (new kernel step)
    outputs:
    - global order k in closed form
  - name: mod_m_normalization
    inputs:
    - integer k and modulus 1000
    purpose: produce the required normalized output N with 0 ≤ N < 1000 and N ≡ k (mod 1000)
    outputs:
    - final answer N
  theorem(s):
  - name: cyclicity_of_Fp_star
    role: ensures (Z/pZ)^× is cyclic, so element orders divide p-1 and are controlled by the prime factorization of p-1
  - name: fermat_little_theorem
    role: guarantees existence of multiplicative orders by a^{p-1} ≡ 1 (mod p) for p ∤ a
  - name: chinese_remainder_theorem
    role: identifies Z/(p1 p2)Z with Z/p1Z × Z/p2Z, enabling the lcm-of-orders certificate
  closest_first_principle_concept_applied:
  - Quadratic characters split the unit group into squares and nonsquares; Euler's criterion converts this split into a forced exponent.
  - For coprime moduli, congruences modulo the product are equivalent to simultaneous congruences; orders compose via an lcm constraint.
solution_signature:
  critical_trick(s):
  - 'Use the coupled constraints p1 ≡ 2 (mod q) and p2 ≡ -2 (mod q) to force a reciprocity split: (q/p1)→(-2/q) and (q/p2)→(2/q).'
  - Exploit the safe-prime structure so each local order is in {r_i,2r_i} and Euler's criterion selects the correct branch.
  - 'New kernel: a CRT-driven lcm certificate to pass from local orders to the global order modulo p1 p2.'
  - Finish by the mandated reduction modulo 1000.
  finish_type: closed_form
computational_context:
  preferred_language: python
  library(s):
  - name: math
    reason: gcd/lcm arithmetic and modular reductions.
  - name: sympy
